fitness-sparse.content:
# The Executive Athlete Sales Call Script

*A complete 45-60 minute conversation guide for qualified prospects*

---

## SECTION 1: INTRODUCTION & RAPPORT (0:00-0:01)

**Marcus:** "Hey [Name], good to connect with you. How's your day going so far?"

[Wait for response]

"Good, good. I saw your application come through and I've got to say, your situation sounds familiar — reminds me of a lot of the executives I work with. Before we dive into the details, how did you hear about what I do?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: Keep this light and conversational. You're establishing that you're a real person, not a sales robot. Don't spend more than 60 seconds here.]

---

## SECTION 2: FRAME SETTING (0:01-0:02)

**Marcus:** "Cool. So here's how I like to do these calls. I'm going to ask you some questions to really understand where you are right now and what you're dealing with. Then, if I think I can actually help you, I'll share what that looks like. If I don't think we're a good fit, I'll be straight with you about that too and point you in the right direction. Sound fair?"

[Wait for agreement]

"Perfect. And just so you know — this isn't some high-pressure pitch. I only work with 12 clients at a time, so I'm genuinely trying to understand if we'd be a good match. I'd rather have that conversation honestly than waste either of our time. Make sense?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: This removes pressure and positions you as selective, not desperate.]

---

## SECTION 3: PROBLEM ISOLATION (0:02-0:03)

**Marcus:** "Alright, so tell me — what's the biggest challenge you're facing with your health and fitness right now? What made you reach out?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: SHUT UP and listen. Do not interrupt. Do not solve yet. Let them talk for as long as they want.]

**Follow-up:** "And how long has this been going on for you?"

---

## SECTION 4: PROBLEM UNDERSTANDING — DEEP PROBING (0:03-0:11)

**Chunking Questions:**

**Marcus:** "When you say [repeat their exact words], what does that actually look like day-to-day? Can you give me a specific example from this past week?"

[Listen to response]

"And what's that costing you right now — in terms of energy? Time? How you feel about yourself?"

**Consequence Questions:**

**Marcus:** "How is this affecting your work performance? Your family time?"

[Listen to response]

"If nothing changes — if you're dealing with this same situation ___ from now — where does that leave you?"

**Feeling Questions:**

**Marcus:** "How does that make you feel when you think about it?"

[Listen to response]

"What's the worst part about this whole situation for you personally?"

**Business Impact Questions:**

**Marcus:** "You mentioned you work [X hours] a week — how is your energy level affecting your decision-making? Your presence in meetings?"

[Listen to response]

"Are there business opportunities you feel like you're not showing up fully for because of how you're feeling physically?"

**Family Impact Questions:**

**Marcus:** "What about at home? How is this affecting your time with your family?"

[Listen to response]

"Is your wife worried about your health? What do your kids see when they look at you?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: This is the MOST IMPORTANT section. Spend 8-10 minutes here. The more pain you uncover, the easier everything else becomes. Use their exact words back to them. Reference the emotional triggers — elevator mirrors, missing kids' games, father's heart attack at 58.]

---

## SECTION 5: DOUBT CREATION (0:11-0:12)

**Marcus:** "So you've been trying to [repeat their current approach] — and how's that been working for you?"

[They'll usually laugh or say "obviously not great"]

"Right. Why do you think [their approach] hasn't worked so far? What do you think has been missing?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: Don't be condescending. You're helping them realize their current path isn't working, not attacking them for trying it.]

---

## SECTION 6: PAST SOLUTION ANALYSIS (0:12-0:15)

**Marcus:** "What else have you tried to solve this? What programs, trainers, or approaches have you invested in?"

[Listen to their list]

"Okay, so you tried [list them back]. What worked? What didn't? And why do you think those didn't stick?"

[Listen to response]

"What's different about your situation now compared to when you tried those things? Are you busier? More motivated? What's changed?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: Listen for patterns. Usually they've tried generic programs that didn't account for their executive lifestyle. Reference this later.]

---

## SECTION 7: SUPPORT ASSESSMENT (0:15-0:17)

**Marcus:** "Who's helping you with this right now? Do you have a trainer, coach, or anyone guiding you through this?"

[Listen to response]

"So you're pretty much figuring this out on your own? How are you making decisions about what to do next — just researching online, or...?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: Most will say they're going it alone. This sets up the need for expert guidance.]

---

## SECTION 8: DESIRE EXPLORATION (0:17-0:17)

**Marcus:** "Alright, let's flip this. If we could wave a magic wand and this was handled — what would your life look like 6 months from now? Walk me through your ideal day."

[Let them dream out loud]

"How would that feel compared to where you are now? Who would you become if this was handled?"

[Listen for their version of "The Executive Athlete"]

"What would it mean to you to feel that confident and energetic again?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: Let them paint their own Heaven Island. The more vividly they describe it, the more motivated they'll be to invest.]

---

## SECTION 9: WHY NOW / COST OF INACTION (0:17-0:21)

**Marcus:** "Let me ask you something. You mentioned you've been dealing with [their problem] for [timeframe]. What has that already cost you?"

[Listen to response]

"And I mean really — in terms of energy, confidence, time with your family, even business performance. What's the real cost been?"

[Listen to response]

"Now, if nothing changes — if you're in this exact same spot ___ from now, maybe even worse — what does that look like? Is that acceptable to you?"

[Listen to response]

"You mentioned your father had a heart attack at 58. How old are you now? How does that timeline feel?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: This should feel like holding up a mirror, not manipulation. Reference their specific fears and consequences.]

---

## SECTION 10: CLARITY TIE-DOWN (0:21-0:23)

**Marcus:** "Let me make sure I'm understanding you correctly. You're dealing with [summarize their problem], you've tried [list their attempts], but nothing has stuck because [their reasons]. What you really want is [their desired outcome], and if this doesn't get handled, you're looking at [consequences]. Does that sound right? Did I miss anything?"

[Wait for confirmation]

"And the biggest frustration is that you know you can figure out complex business problems, but this one has you stuck. Fair?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: If they say "yes, exactly" — you've built trust. If they correct you, adjust and re-confirm.]

---

## SECTION 11: DISCOVERY COMMITMENT (0:23-0:24)

**Marcus:** "Based on everything you've shared, I actually think I can help. Would you like to hear how I'd approach this?"

[Wait for yes]

"Cool. Let me walk you through exactly what I'd do if we worked together."

[DELIVERY NOTE: They'll almost always say yes. If they hesitate, that's fine — enthusiasm comes during the pitch.]

---

## SECTION 12: TRANSITION TO PITCH (0:24-0:26)

**Marcus:** "So here's what I've noticed from everything you've shared. The reason the Peloton and the trainers and all that hasn't worked isn't because you lack discipline. You run a company — you've got plenty of discipline."

"The problem is you've been trying to use systems designed for people who don't work 60-hour weeks, don't travel constantly, and don't have the decision fatigue you deal with every day."

"It's like trying to use a roadmap for New York to navigate Los Angeles. Both are cities, both have streets, but you need the right map for your specific territory."

"What I've built is specifically designed for executives like you. It's called The Performance Stack, and here's how it works..."

[DELIVERY NOTE: Connect their failures to system problems, not personal problems. This removes shame and creates hope.]

---

## SECTION 13: THE PITCH — 3-PHASE SOLUTION (0:26-0:35)

**Marcus:** "The Executive Athlete Program has three phases, and each one builds on the last."

**"Phase 1: Assessment"**

"First thing we do is get your custom baseline. We're talking 3 video calls plus a complete bloodwork review. This isn't some cookie-cutter assessment — we're looking at your specific health history, your current fitness level, your travel schedule, everything."

"Remember when you said [reference their specific situation]? This is where we figure out exactly what your body needs and what's realistic given your actual life, not some fantasy where you have unlimited time."

"The outcome is a complete picture of where you're starting from and a program built specifically for you."

**"Phase 2: Rebuild"**

"Next, we rebuild your foundation. This is 8 weeks of in-home strength training plus a nutrition protocol that actually works with business dinners and travel."

"You mentioned [reference their travel/schedule challenges] — this phase is designed around that. You're training in your home office between meetings, not driving to some gym. The nutrition works whether you're in Austin or Atlanta."

"The outcome is your first 15-20 pound loss plus a strength baseline that makes you feel powerful again. This is where you start seeing the guy in the mirror you actually want to be."

**"Phase 3: Scale"**

"Finally, we scale it up. 12 weeks of progressive loading plus travel protocols that work in any hotel gym or even just a hotel room."

"This is where you go from [reference their current state] to [reference their desired outcome]. You've got a system that travels with you, adapts to your schedule, and gets you the transformation you want."

"The outcome is becoming The Executive Athlete — a high performer who takes his body as seriously as his P&L."

[DELIVERY NOTE: Throughout the pitch, reference THEIR specific words from discovery. Make it feel custom, not scripted.]

---

## SECTION 14: TEMPERATURE CHECK (0:36-0:39)

**Marcus:** "So on a scale of 1 to 10, how does this feel so far?"

**If 7+:** "What would make it a 10 for you?"

**If 5-6:** "What's holding you back? What doesn't feel right?"

**If below 5:** "Sounds like this might not be the right fit, and that's totally fine. What specifically doesn't resonate?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: The temperature check reveals hidden objections. Handle them before moving forward.]

---

## SECTION 15: TRANSITION TO CLOSE (0:39-0:40)

**Marcus:** "Great. So would you like to hear what it looks like to get started?"

[Wait for yes]

"Perfect. Let me walk you through exactly what you get and how this works."

---

## SECTION 16: DELIVERABLES & SUPPORT (0:40-0:41)

**Marcus:** "Here's exactly what you get: Custom training programs designed for your home office setup. Nutrition protocols that work with your business travel. Weekly video reviews where I personally look at your form and give you feedback. Biweekly one-on-one calls where we strategize around your specific challenges. Private Slack access so you can reach me directly when questions come up. And hotel protocols for any travel situation."

"The whole thing is delivered through a private client portal that works on your phone, tablet, or computer. Whether you're in your office or traveling for business, you've got everything you need."

"You'll never be stuck wondering what to do next. Any questions about how it works?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: Be specific about what they get. This isn't theoretical — it's practical support.]

---

## SECTION 17: INVESTMENT REVEAL (0:41-0:42)

**Marcus:** "The investment for The Executive Athlete Program is $12,000 for 6 months. You can also do 6 monthly payments of $2,100 if that works better for your cash flow."

[PAUSE. Do not fill the silence.]

"And here's my guarantee: If you don't lose 20 pounds in the first 90 days while following the protocol, I work with you free until you do."

"To put that in perspective — you mentioned you're spending about $500 a month on [their current waste]. This pays for itself just in stopping that waste, not to mention what it's worth to actually get your energy and confidence back."

[DELIVERY NOTE: State the price confidently and stop talking. Let them process.]

---

## SECTION 18: OBJECTION HANDLING

**Objection: "I need to think about it"**

**Marcus:** "I totally understand. Can I ask — what specifically do you need to think about? Is it the investment, the time commitment, or something else?"

[Listen to real objection]

"In my experience, 'I need to think about it' usually means there's a specific concern I haven't addressed. What is it?"

**Objection: "I can't afford it"**

**Marcus:** "I hear you. Let me ask — is it that the money literally doesn't exist, or that you're not sure if it's worth the investment?"

**If literal:** "Would the payment plan make this possible for you?"

**If value:** "What would it be worth to you to go from [current state] to [desired state] in the next 6 months? You mentioned [specific cost of staying stuck] — how much is that costing you per year?"

**Objection: "I don't have time to train"**

**Marcus:** "That's exactly why I created this system. You're not finding 2-hour blocks for the gym. You're doing 15-minute sessions in your home office between meetings. Dan M. travels 2 weeks per month and hasn't missed a session. The system works with your schedule, not against it."

**Objection: "Online coaching can't replace in-person"**

**Marcus:** "Here's the thing — Dan achieved better results with online coaching than he did in 3 years of trying in-person approaches. Why? Because I understand your lifestyle. I'm not going to cancel when you have a business emergency. The weekly video reviews give you better form feedback than most trainers provide in person."

**Objection: "I've tried this before and it didn't stick"**

**Marcus:** "Right, because you were trying to use systems designed for people with unlimited time. That's like blaming a fish for not being able to climb a tree. You weren't lacking discipline — you were using the wrong system for your environment. This is built specifically for executives who work 60-hour weeks."

**Objection: "I need to talk to my wife"**

**Marcus:** "Absolutely — it's important to be aligned. What specifically do you think she'd want to know? Is it the investment, the time commitment, or something else?"

[Listen to response]

"Would it help if I sent you a summary of everything we discussed that you could share with her? Or would she want to jump on a quick call with both of us?"

[DELIVERY NOTE: Never argue with objections. Understand them, validate them, then address the real concern underneath.]

---

## SECTION 19: THE CLOSE (0:42+)

**If ready:**

**Marcus:** "Awesome. Let me get you set up right now. I'll send you the enrollment link and we can get your first assessment call scheduled. When works better for you — mornings or afternoons?"

**If needs time:**

**Marcus:** "No pressure at all. I'll send you a summary of everything we discussed. When's a good time to follow up — tomorrow or the day after?"

**If no:**

**Marcus:** "I respect that. This isn't right for everyone, and that's fine. If anything changes, you know where to find me. In the meantime, here's what I'd recommend: [offer specific free advice based on their situation]."

[DELIVERY NOTE: Never beg, negotiate on price, or create fake urgency. Be professional and confident.]

---

## SECTION 20: POST-CALL SUMMARY

**Template for follow-up:**

- **Prospect name:**
- **Key problem identified:**
- **What resonated most:**
- **Objections raised:**
- **Outcome (closed / follow-up / not a fit):**
- **Follow-up date:**
- **Notes:**

---

### DELIVERY PRINCIPLES

- **Discovery is everything:** Spend 50% of the call understanding their situation
- **Use their words:** Reference their specific language throughout the pitch
- **Don't chase:** If they're not ready, respect that and move on
- **Be human:** This is a conversation between two professionals, not a sales presentation
- **Listen more than you talk:** The prospect should do 60% of the talking
- **Handle objections with curiosity:** "Help me understand..." not "Let me convince you..."
- **Never apologize for the price:** You're providing massive value to successful people

fitness-sparse.content:
# How Male Executives Are Getting in the Best Shape of Their Lives Without Leaving Their Home Office

## HERO SECTION

**Headline:** "Finally Become The Executive Athlete — A High Performer Who Takes His Body as Seriously as His P&L"

**Subheadline:** If you're a male executive making $300K+ who's tired of catching your reflection in elevator mirrors and feeling embarrassed, exhausted by 3pm, and dreading your next physical — while your Peloton collects dust and every trainer you've hired doesn't understand your 60-hour weeks — there's finally a system built for your reality.

**Primary CTA:** Apply for The Executive Athlete Program

---

## PROBLEM SECTION

### The Corner Office Couch Problem

You catch your reflection in the elevator mirror each morning and look away. 

By 3pm you're running on fumes, skipping your son's baseball games because you're too tired. Your Peloton collects dust while you pay that monthly fee, and every trainer you've hired gave you the same cookie-cutter program that doesn't account for your 2-week business trips or 60-hour weeks.

You're watching younger VPs out-lift you at company retreats while secretly fearing you'll have a heart attack before 55 like your father did at 58.

Here's what's really happening:

• **No time to train** — Generic programs assume you have 2-hour blocks every morning
• **Previous trainers don't understand your schedule** — They cancel when you have business emergencies  
• **Cookie-cutter programs fail** — Nothing accounts for business travel or client dinners
• **Energy crashes by 3pm** — You're running on caffeine instead of real vitality
• **Wasted money on unused equipment** — That $500/month on memberships and gear you never touch

Sound familiar?

You're not lacking discipline. You're not broken. You're just using systems designed for people who don't run companies.

---

## SOLUTION SECTION

### The Performance Stack System

Here's what actually works: **The Performance Stack** — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of your 60-hour work week.

This isn't another generic program. This is built specifically for executives who travel 2 weeks per month, work 60-hour weeks, and need results that don't disappear the moment work gets demanding.

**How The Executive Athlete Program Works:**

**Phase 1: Assessment (Month 1)**
We create your custom baseline through 3 video calls plus bloodwork review. No guessing about what your body needs — we get a complete picture of your current health status and build a program that actually fits your schedule.

**Phase 2: Rebuild (Months 2-3)**  
8 weeks of in-home strength training plus nutrition protocols that work with business travel. You'll lose your first 15-20 pounds while building real strength — all from your home office between meetings.

**Phase 3: Scale (Months 4-6)**
12 weeks of progressive loading plus travel protocols. You complete your transformation and build a maintenance system that works whether you're in Austin or Atlanta.

**Why This Works When Everything Else Failed:**
You wouldn't run your business on willpower alone — you have systems, processes, and accountability measures. Your health deserves the same systematic approach you use to generate millions in revenue.

---

## RESULTS SECTION

### The Executive Athlete Transformation

**What You Get:**
Lose 30 pounds in 6 months without missing a single business meeting. Wake up energized and maintain that energy throughout 12-hour workdays. Take off your shirt at the beach without sucking in. Be the executive who finally cracked the code on getting in the best shape of his life.

**Real Results from Real Executives:**

Dan M., SaaS CEO, was burned out, 42 pounds over target, and hadn't trained in 3 years. After 6 months in The Executive Athlete Program: Lost 42 pounds, squats 2x bodyweight, travels 2 weeks per month and stays on track. "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

Rick P., private equity partner, had a prediabetic A1C of 6.2. After our 6-month program: A1C dropped to 5.4, lost 28 pounds. "My doctor couldn't believe the numbers."

**Your Complete Support System:**
- Custom training programs designed for your home office setup
- Business-travel nutrition strategies that work with client dinners  
- Weekly video reviews where I personally check your form
- Biweekly 1-on-1 calls to strategize around your specific challenges
- Private Slack access for real-time support
- Hotel gym protocols for any travel situation

---

## ABOUT SECTION

### Why I Built This for Executives Like You

My name is Marcus Reed. Over the last 11 years, I've coached 340 clients and learned something important: conventional fitness doesn't work for people who run companies.

In 2021 I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even after lease and payroll. I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting clients. Then a client asked if I could train him over Zoom during COVID. I realized I could help just as many people without a physical location.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online. Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs using The Performance Stack system.

I do this because I was the fat kid nobody believed in, and now I help people who've given up on themselves prove their own critics wrong. Every successful executive deserves a system that works with their life, not against it.

**Credentials:** NASM CPT, Precision Nutrition L1

---

## NOT FOR YOU SECTION

### This is NOT For You If...

Listen, I need to be direct about something. This program isn't for everyone.

**Don't apply if you:**
- Want a quick fix or magic pill solution
- Expect results without following a structured system for 6 months  
- Think online coaching can't be as effective as in-person training
- Are looking for the cheapest option available
- Believe you can figure this out yourself with more willpower

If that's you, please don't apply. You'll waste both our time.

---

## THIS IS FOR YOU SECTION

### This IS For You If...

**Apply if you:**
- Are a male executive age 40-55 making $300K+ who runs his own company
- Haven't prioritized your health in 3-5 years and have tried multiple approaches that failed
- Travel frequently for business and need a system that works from hotel rooms
- Are tired of cookie-cutter programs that don't account for your 60-hour work weeks  
- Are ready to invest in a system that treats your body transformation as seriously as your business success

You're an action taker who knows that the right system beats willpower every time. You understand ROI and you're ready to invest in your most important asset — your health and energy.

---

## FINAL CTA SECTION

### Stop Being The Corner Office Couch. Become The Executive Athlete.

Right now you have two choices:

Keep doing what you've been doing — buying equipment you don't use, hiring trainers you cancel on, telling yourself you'll start "after this quarter ends" — and stay The Corner Office Couch who catches his reflection and looks away.

Or draw a line in the sand today and become The Executive Athlete — the high performer who takes his body as seriously as his P&L.

**The Investment:** $12,000 for 6 months (or 6 monthly payments of $2,100)

**My Guarantee:** If you don't lose 20 pounds in the first 90 days while following the protocol, I work with you free until you do.

**Important:** I only work with 12 active clients at a time. Enrollment opens only when an existing client graduates.

**Next Step:** Go to reedperformance.co/apply right now and fill out the application. I'll personally review every application and if you're a good fit, we'll schedule a call to discuss your specific situation.

The question is: Will you still be catching your reflection in that elevator mirror 6 months from now feeling embarrassed and exhausted?

Or will you be the executive who finally cracked the code?

**[Apply for The Executive Athlete Program →](https://reedperformance.co/apply)**

fitness-sparse.content:
# About Marcus Reed

## You're Not Broken — Your System Is

Here's the serious question every successful executive needs to ask: How long are you going to keep catching your reflection in that elevator mirror each morning and looking away?

Because here's what I know about you — you built a company that generates serious revenue, you make decisions that affect dozens of people, and you solve complex problems every single day. But when it comes to your own health, you're stuck using systems designed for people who don't run businesses.

I'm Marcus Reed, and for the last 11 years, I've helped 340 executives get back in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office. But my story didn't start there.

## From Gym Owner to Executive Specialist

In 2021, I was The Corner Office Couch myself — training clients 12 hours a day at my gym in Austin and still barely breaking even after lease and payroll. I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting the very people I was supposed to help. The irony wasn't lost on me: I was a fitness professional who couldn't even take care of his own health.

Sound familiar?

Then COVID hit, and a client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes out of desperation, but something clicked. I realized I could help just as many people — maybe more — without the limitations of a physical location. More importantly, I could finally design programs that worked with executive schedules instead of against them.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online. That's when I developed The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week. Not the fantasy of having 2 hours every morning, but the reality of back-to-back meetings, business travel, and decision fatigue.

Now I help executives become The Executive Athlete — high performers who take their bodies as seriously as their P&L statements. Because I was the fat kid nobody believed in, and now I help people who've given up on themselves prove their own critics wrong.

## What I Believe

These aren't just nice ideas — they're the principles that separate executives who transform from those who stay stuck:

**Generic fitness programs are designed for people who don't run companies.** When a program assumes you have unlimited time and a predictable schedule, it's already failed. You need a system built for 60-hour weeks, business travel, and the reality that your Tuesday might get blown up by a client emergency.

**Your problem isn't willpower — it's that you've been using the wrong system for your environment.** You wouldn't blame a fish for not climbing a tree. You've been trying to force systems designed for people with gym memberships and flexible schedules into a life that includes quarterly board meetings and red-eye flights.

**Online coaching beats in-person training for busy executives.** I'm not going to cancel when you have a business crisis. I'm not going to give you a cookie-cutter program because I don't understand your industry. The weekly video reviews give you better feedback than most trainers provide standing next to you.

**The best investment you can make is in your health and energy.** You already know this in business — you invest in systems that multiply your effectiveness. Your health is the foundation of every decision you make, every meeting you attend, and every strategy you execute.

**Systems beat motivation every time.** You don't run your business on willpower alone. You have processes, KPIs, and accountability measures. Your health deserves the same systematic approach you use to generate revenue.

**The best time to start was when you first noticed the problem — the next best time is now.** Every month you delay is another month closer to the heart attack your father had at 58. Plus you're wasting money on unused Peloton memberships and canceled trainers.

## The Track Record

In 11 years of training, I've worked with 340 clients and earned my NASM CPT and Precision Nutrition L1 certifications. But the numbers that matter most are these: out of my last 60 executive clients, 52 achieved their transformation goals. The first measurable wins usually show up within the first 3 weeks — clients drop 5-8 pounds and feel noticeably stronger before month 1 ends.

Dan M., a SaaS CEO, lost 42 pounds in 6 months while traveling 2 weeks per month. Rick P., a private equity partner, dropped his A1C from 6.2 to 5.4 and lost 28 pounds in the same timeframe. These aren't anomalies — they're what happens when you use a system designed for your actual life.

## The Personal Side

When I'm not helping executives reclaim their health, you'll find me running the trails around Austin, reading everything I can get my hands on about performance optimization, or perfecting my barbecue technique in the backyard. My wife and two kids keep me grounded and remind me daily why this work matters — not just for the executives I serve, but for the families who get their energy and presence back.

I live the system I teach. I train from my home office between client calls, I follow the same travel protocols I give my clients, and I track the same metrics I ask them to monitor. This isn't theoretical — it's the daily reality of someone who understands that your health has to work with your life, not against it.

## Ready to Stop Making Excuses?

If you're tired of catching your reflection and feeling embarrassed, if you're done being exhausted by 3pm, and if you're ready to stop wasting money on solutions that don't account for your reality — then let's talk.

The Executive Athlete Program isn't for everyone. It's for male executives who run their own companies, make serious money, and are ready to invest in a system that actually works with their 60-hour weeks and constant travel.

If that's you, go to [reedperformance.co/apply](https://reedperformance.co/apply) and fill out the application. I'll personally review it, and if you're a good fit, we'll discuss whether this program can help you finally become The Executive Athlete you know you can be.

The question is simple: Will you still be avoiding that elevator mirror 6 months from now, or will you finally be the executive who cracked the code on getting in the best shape of his life?

# Welcome Email Sequence for Reed Performance

---

## EMAIL 1: Welcome + Delivery
**Send:** Immediately after opt-in  
**Purpose:** Deliver lead magnet, set expectations, build immediate trust

**Subject Line:** Your 15-minute workout is here (plus what's coming next)
**Alternative:** The Executive's 15-Minute Workout + 6 more emails

**Preview Text:** Download your workout guide and see what's coming this week

**Body Copy:**

You just downloaded The Executive's 15-Minute Workout — it's designed specifically for executives who need results without leaving their home office.

Here's what you can expect over the next week:

Tomorrow, I'm going to share the story of how I went from training 12 hours a day at a failing gym to helping executives get in the best shape of their lives from their home office.

Then I'll show you why every fitness approach you've tried has failed (hint: it's not your fault).

And by the end of the week, you'll understand exactly why The Performance Stack works when everything else doesn't.

Quick question while you're here: What's the biggest obstacle keeping you from getting back in shape right now?

Hit reply and let me know. I read every response.

Marcus

P.S. That 15-minute workout? Try it tomorrow morning before your first meeting. You'll be surprised how much energy it gives you for the rest of the day.

**CTA:** Hit reply with your biggest obstacle

---

## EMAIL 2: Origin Story → Hell Island to Heaven Island
**Send:** Day 1-2  
**Purpose:** Build trust through shared struggle, show transformation is possible

**Subject Line:** How I went from 12-hour gym days to online success
**Alternative:** The day I realized everything I knew about fitness was wrong

**Preview Text:** My journey from burnt-out gym owner to executive fitness specialist

**Body Copy:**

In 2021, I was that guy catching his reflection in the gym mirrors and not liking what I saw.

I was training clients 12 hours a day at my Austin gym, barely breaking even after lease and payroll. I was gaining weight, resenting my clients, and burning out fast.

Sound familiar?

I was exhausted by mid-afternoon, skipping meals, and telling myself I'd focus on my own health "once the business got stable."

The irony wasn't lost on me — I was a personal trainer who couldn't even take care of himself.

Then COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, mainly because I needed the money.

That single session changed everything.

I realized I could help just as many people without the overhead, the scheduling conflicts, or the limitations of a physical location.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online with customized home programs.

Now I coach executives remotely with systems that actually fit their 60-hour work weeks. I'm in the best shape of my life, and my clients are getting better results than they ever did in traditional gyms.

The lesson? Sometimes the solution isn't working harder — it's working smarter with the right system.

Tomorrow I'll show you why every fitness program you've tried has set you up to fail from day one.

Marcus

**CTA:** None (story-building email)

---

## EMAIL 3: Core Belief #1 — Your Current Method Is the Problem
**Send:** Day 3-4  
**Purpose:** Challenge false belief about willpower, introduce systems thinking

**Subject Line:** Why your Peloton is judging you (and it's not your fault)
**Alternative:** The real reason you can't stick to workout programs

**Preview Text:** It's not about discipline — it's about the wrong system

**Body Copy:**

Here's the serious question every successful executive needs to ask:

How do you think your health is going to work if you keep using systems designed for people who don't run companies?

You're not lacking willpower. You're trying to use a roadmap for New York City to navigate Los Angeles.

Think about it:

You buy a Peloton designed for people with 2-hour morning blocks.

You hire trainers who expect you to show up at the same time every day.

You download MyFitnessPal assuming you'll have time to log every meal between board meetings.

Then when your 60-hour work week and business travel make it impossible to stick to these programs, you blame yourself for lacking discipline.

But here's what I want you to understand: You don't run your business on willpower alone.

You have systems, processes, and accountability measures. You track KPIs, have regular check-ins, and adjust based on data.

Your health deserves the same systematic approach you use to generate revenue.

The problem isn't your discipline. The problem is that you've been trying to force systems designed for people with unlimited time into a life that demands strategic efficiency.

Tomorrow I'll show you what the real problem actually is (and why it's been hiding in plain sight).

Marcus

**CTA:** None (belief-shifting email)

---

## EMAIL 4: Core Belief #2 — The Real Problem Is Not What You Think
**Send:** Day 5-6  
**Purpose:** Redefine the real problem, introduce The Performance Stack

**Subject Line:** Your real problem isn't what you think it is
**Alternative:** The hidden problem that's sabotaging your fitness

**Preview Text:** Why "no time to train" is just a symptom of something deeper

**Body Copy:**

You think your problem is "I don't have time to train."

But that's just a symptom.

Your real problem is that you don't have a system that fits the reality of a 60-hour work week.

Here's the difference:

Most executives try to solve the time problem by finding more time. They wake up earlier, skip lunch, or promise to work out "after this busy season."

But you already know this in business — when a sales process isn't working, you don't blame your sales team's character. You fix the process.

When a marketing campaign fails, you don't question your team's willpower. You adjust the strategy.

The system determines the results, not the person's moral fiber.

That's why I developed The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of your actual work week.

Layer 1: Training that happens in your home office between meetings
Layer 2: Nutrition that works during client dinners and business travel  
Layer 3: Recovery that's built into your evening routine

Instead of fighting against your schedule, you work with it.

Instead of needing 2-hour gym blocks, you need 15-minute focused sessions.

Instead of meal prep on Sundays, you have protocols for hotel rooms and conference centers.

The system runs itself. You just show up.

Tomorrow I'll show you proof that this actually works for executives just like you.

Marcus

**CTA:** None (education email)

---

## EMAIL 5: Core Belief #5 — Why This Approach Works
**Send:** Day 7-8  
**Purpose:** Provide proof through case studies, build confidence in method

**Subject Line:** "Marcus is the only trainer who gets what my life is like"
**Alternative:** How a SaaS CEO lost 42 lbs while traveling 2 weeks per month

**Preview Text:** Real results from executives who work 60-hour weeks

**Body Copy:**

Dan M. runs a SaaS company. Burned out, 42 pounds over target, hadn't trained in 3 years.

He'd tried Peloton, local trainers, fitness apps — nothing stuck with his travel schedule and 60-hour weeks.

Sound familiar?

Dan applied The Performance Stack system. Within the first 3 weeks, he dropped 5-8 pounds and felt noticeably stronger before month 1 ended.

Six months later: 42 pounds lost, squats 2x bodyweight, travels 2 weeks per month and stays on track.

"Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

Then there's Rick P., private equity partner. Prediabetic A1C of 6.2, frustrated with programs that didn't work with his demanding schedule.

Same system. Six months later: A1C dropped to 5.4, lost 28 pounds.

"My doctor couldn't believe the numbers."

Here's what both Dan and Rick discovered:

The problem was never their discipline. It was that they were using systems designed for people living completely different lives.

The Performance Stack works because it's built for the reality of running a company — not some fantasy where you have unlimited time and zero business demands.

It's not about finding more time. It's about using the time you have more strategically.

Tomorrow I'll show you what happens if you keep doing what you're doing now.

Marcus

**CTA:** None (proof-building email)

---

## EMAIL 6: Core Belief #4 — The Cost of Inaction + Investment Reframe
**Send:** Day 9-10  
**Purpose:** Create urgency through cost of inaction, reframe investment

**Subject Line:** The real cost of waiting until "after this quarter"
**Alternative:** What happens if you do nothing for the next 6 months?

**Preview Text:** Every month of delay has a price tag

**Body Copy:**

Let me paint you a picture of what the next 6 months look like if nothing changes:

You'll still be catching your reflection in the elevator mirror every morning and looking away.

You'll still be exhausted by 3pm, running on caffeine and willpower.

You'll still be paying that monthly Peloton fee while it collects dust.

You'll still be dreading your next physical, knowing the numbers are trending in the wrong direction.

Here's what that actually costs you:

Every month of delay = another month closer to the heart attack your father had at 58. Plus around $500 per month wasted on unused Peloton memberships and canceled trainers.

But the real cost isn't financial.

It's missing your son's baseball games because you're too tired.

It's avoiding photos at family events.

It's watching younger VPs out-lift you at company retreats.

It's the quiet fear that you're following the same health trajectory that took your father too early.

Now contrast that with this:

Six months from now, you wake up energized and maintain that energy throughout 12-hour workdays.

You've built a system that travels with you — training from hotel rooms, eating strategically during client dinners, staying consistent whether you're home or on the road.

You're stronger and leaner than you've been in decades. Your team notices you're sharper in meetings.

You take your body as seriously as your P&L, because you know your health is the foundation of everything else you've built.

The Executive Athlete Program is $12,000 for 6 months. That's what you'd spend on a single business trip, but with returns that compound daily.

The question isn't whether you can afford it. The question is whether you can afford to stay where you are.

Tomorrow I'll show you exactly how this works and who it's for.

Marcus

**CTA:** None (urgency-building email)

---

## EMAIL 7: The Invitation → Aspiring Identity Sell

**Purpose:** Full offer presentation, sell the identity transformation

There are two types of successful executives:

## SECTION 1: MIDDLE OF FUNNEL (MOF) — 93+ Angles

### A. Identity & Mindset (13 angles)

|---|---|---|---|---|---|

When you become The Executive Athlete, you attract clients who take their bodies seriously. |
| 2 | If you hesitate to invest in your health, your team will hesitate to invest in their performance. | 4 | Universal Analogy | Reel | Leadership means modeling the behavior you want to see. | 3 | How you spend your energy signals who you are — exhausted by 3pm or energized all day? | 3 | Personal Story | Post | Most executives burn through energy like it's unlimited, then wonder why they crash. | 4 | You become who you surround yourself with — are you around executives who make excuses or ones who make results? | 1 | Authority Reference | Carousel | Jim Rohn's principle about being the average of the five people you spend time with applies to health. fitness-sparse.content:
# GHL Chat/SMS Automation: Executive Fitness Transformation

*For Marcus Reed / Reed Performance*

---

## AUTOMATION OVERVIEW

**Target Audience:** Male executives age 40-55 who run their own companies, make $300K+, and haven't prioritized their health in years.

**Qualification Criteria:**
- Company revenue: $500K-$5M annually
- Personal income: $300K+ annually  
- Age: 40-___ old
- Current weight: 25+ pounds over target
- Ready to invest $12,000 in transformation

**Flow Purpose:** Qualify prospects, deliver value, handle objections, and book application calls for The Executive Athlete Program.

---

## QUALIFICATION QUESTIONS

1. **Revenue Qualifier:** "What's your company's annual revenue? (A) Under $500K (B) $500K-$2M (C) $2M-$5M (D) Over $5M"

2. **Health Priority:** "How long has it been since you consistently prioritized your health? (A) Less than ___ (B) 1-___ (C) 3-5 years (D) Over 5 years"

3. **Investment Readiness:** "If you found a system that guaranteed results, what's the most you'd invest in getting back in the best shape of your life? (A) Under $2K (B) $2K-$___ (C) $___-$___ (D) $___+"

4. **Schedule Reality:** "How many hours per week do you typically work? (A) Under 40 (B) 40-50 (C) 50-60 (D) Over 60"

5. **Commitment Level:** "If accepted into a 6-month program, could you commit to 15-minute daily sessions from your home office? (A) Definitely yes (B) Probably yes (C) Maybe (D) Probably not"

---

## MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS

- Company revenue: $500K+ annually
- Personal income: $300K+ annually
- Age: 40-___ old
- Works 50+ hours per week
- 3+ years since consistent health focus
- Ready to invest $___+ in transformation
- Can commit to 6-month program

---

#### STEP 1: Initial Response & Qualification Setup

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact sends DM with keyword or responds to lead magnet

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact responds: → Go to Step 2
> - IF no response after 2 hours: → Go to Step 15 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: executive-fitness-lead]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: New Lead]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Hey there! I see you're interested in getting back in shape without leaving your home office.

Quick question — are you a male executive who runs your own company and makes $300K+? 

Just want to make sure I can actually help before we go further.

Reply YES if that's you, or NO if not.

---

#### STEP 2: Revenue Qualification

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact replies "YES" to Step 1

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact answers A: → Go to Step 16 (Disqualify - too small)
> - IF contact answers B, C, or D: → Go to Step 3
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 15

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: qualified-executive]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. I work specifically with successful executives who understand the value of expert systems.

What's your company's annual revenue?

A) Under $500K
B) $500K-$2M  
C) $2M-$5M
D) Over $5M

Just reply with the letter that fits.

---

#### STEP 3: Health Priority Assessment

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact replies B, C, or D to Step 2

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact answers A or B: → Go to Step 17 (Different approach needed)
> - IF contact answers C or D: → Go to Step 4
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 15

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: revenue-qualified]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Got it. Here's the real question...

How long has it been since you consistently prioritized your health?

A) Less than ___
B) 1-___  
C) 3-5 years
D) Over 5 years

The guys who get the best results are usually the ones who've been putting this off the longest.

---

#### STEP 4: Investment Readiness Check

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact replies C or D to Step 3

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact answers A or B: → Go to Step 18 (Price objection sequence)
> - IF contact answers C or D: → Go to Step 5
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 15

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: health-neglected-3plus-years]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

That's exactly what I expected. Most executives I work with are in the same boat.

Here's the thing — you've probably tried Pelotons, local trainers, apps. None of it stuck because none of it was designed for someone who works 60-hour weeks and travels constantly.

If you found a system that guaranteed results and was built specifically for executives like you, what's the most you'd invest in getting back in the best shape of your life?

A) Under $2K
B) $2K-$___
C) $___-$___  
D) $___+

Be honest. Your health is either a priority or it isn't.

---

#### STEP 5: Schedule Reality Check

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact replies C or D to Step 4

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact answers A or B: → Go to Step 19 (Different program needed)
> - IF contact answers C or D: → Go to Step 6
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 15

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: investment-ready-10k-plus]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Good. You understand that real transformation requires real investment.

How many hours per week do you typically work?

A) Under 40
B) 40-50
C) 50-60
D) Over 60

I need to know because my system is specifically designed for executives who can't just "find more time" in their schedule.

---

#### STEP 6: Commitment Assessment

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact replies C or D to Step 5

**Time Delay:** None

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact answers A or B: → Go to Step 20 (Commitment concern)
> - IF contact answers C or D: → Go to Step 7
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 15

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: works-50plus-hours]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. That's exactly who this system was built for.

Final question: If accepted into a 6-month program, could you commit to 15-minute daily sessions from your home office? No gym, no travel required.

A) Definitely yes
B) Probably yes  
C) Maybe
D) Probably not

This isn't about having time — it's about making the decision that your health matters as much as your P&L.

---

Here's what most executives don't understand: You don't have a willpower problem. You have a system problem.

You've been trying to use fitness programs designed for people who don't run companies, don't travel 2 weeks per month, and don't work 60-hour weeks.

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: interested-in-system]

It's a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of your actual work week.

Layer 1: 15-minute strength sessions you do in your home office
Layer 2: Business-travel nutrition that works with client dinners  
Layer 3: Recovery protocols built into your evening routine

No gym memberships. No 2-hour workouts. No meal prep on Sundays.

Just a system that works with your reality, not against it.

Dan M., SaaS CEO, lost 42 pounds in 6 months using this while traveling 2 weeks per month. Never missed a business meeting.

Make sense?

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: understands-system]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Rick P., private equity partner, had an A1C of 6.2 (prediabetic). His doctor told him he was heading for diabetes.

6 months later: A1C dropped to 5.4, lost 28 pounds. His doctor couldn't believe the numbers.

The key? We didn't try to change his schedule. We built a system around it.

Most executives waste money on Pelotons they never use and trainers they constantly cancel on. That's about $500 per month down the drain.

Every month you delay is another month closer to the heart attack your father had at 58. Plus you're wasting money on solutions that don't work.

Ready to see how this actually works?

> - IF contact asks about price: → Go to Step 23 (Investment discussion)
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 15

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: wants-details]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

The Executive Athlete Program has 3 phases:

**Phase 1 - Assessment (Month 1):**
3 video calls + bloodwork review = custom baseline
No guessing. We know exactly where you're starting.

**Phase 2 - Rebuild (Months 2-3):**  
8 weeks of home strength training + nutrition protocol
First 15-20 pounds come off, strength baseline established

**Phase 3 - Scale (Months 4-6):**
12 weeks of progressive loading + travel protocols  
Complete transformation + maintenance system that travels

Within the first 3 weeks, clients drop 5-8 pounds and feel noticeably stronger before month 1 ends.

By month 6: 25-45 pounds lost, strength baseline doubled, energy consistent from 6am to 8pm.

Questions so far?

✓ Custom training programs for your home office setup

Starting to see why this works when everything else fails?

You can do 6 monthly payments of $2,100 if that works better for your cash flow.

Compare that to what you're spending now: $500/month on unused Pelotons and canceled trainers = $___over 6 months with zero results.

This is $12,000 for a complete transformation that lasts.

What's the real cost of staying where you are for another 6 months?

Here's the reality: Most executives who read this will do nothing.

They'll keep catching their reflection in elevator mirrors feeling embarrassed. Keep crashing by 3pm. Keep dreading their next physical.

But you're not most executives. You're here asking the right questions.

The application is at reedperformance.co/apply

Fair warning: I'm selective. This only works for executives who are serious about transformation, not just curious about it.

You can stay The Corner Office Couch, or you can become The Executive Athlete.

What's it going to be?

Talk soon.

Your health is what shows up to every meeting, makes every decision, executes every strategy.

What's really holding you back from making this a priority?

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

But here's what I've learned in 11 years of coaching: The executives who transform their lives make decisions quickly and stick to them.

The ones who "think about it

fitness-sparse.content:
# Reed Performance Facebook Content Library

## Post 1: IDENTITY (MOF)
**Type:** Identity Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Engaged with fitness content

There are two types of male executives making $300K+:

**The Corner Office Couch** — catches his reflection in the elevator mirror each morning and looks away. By 3pm he's running on fumes, skipping his son's baseball games because he's too tired. His Peloton collects dust while he pays that monthly fee.

**The Executive Athlete** — walks into every room with the quiet confidence of a man who's built something impressive in business AND his body. He's got the energy to be fully present for his kids and the strength to handle whatever his business throws at him.

The Corner Office Couch thinks he'll start "after this quarter ends."

The Executive Athlete knows his health is the foundation that makes everything else possible.

Which one are you becoming?

---

## Post 2: VALUE (TOF)
**Type:** Value Post | **Funnel Stage:** TOF | **Retargeting:** N

Most executives think they need 2-hour gym blocks to get in shape.

Here's what actually works:

15-minute training sessions between meetings in your home office.

**Try this today:**
- 5 minutes: bodyweight squats, push-ups, planks
- 5 minutes: resistance band work (keeps in desk drawer)
- 5 minutes: mobility and breathing

Your body adapts to consistent stimulus, not perfect stimulus.

The executive who trains 15 minutes daily beats the one who plans 2-hour sessions he never does.

What's been your experience with short vs long workouts?

---

## Post 3: BELIEF-SHIFTING (MOF)
**Type:** Belief-Shifting Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Viewed health content

You think your problem is lack of time.

It's actually lack of system design.

Here's the truth: You wouldn't run your business on willpower alone — you have systems, processes, and accountability measures. You track KPIs, have regular check-ins, and adjust based on data.

Your health deserves the same systematic approach you use to generate millions in revenue.

The executive who relies on motivation to hit the gym fails when work gets busy.

The executive who builds systems that sync with his work rhythm stays consistent regardless of what's happening at the office.

Stop trying to force generic programs into your 60-hour weeks.

Start building systems that work with your reality, not against it.

---

## Post 4: STORY (MOF)
**Type:** Story Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Business/entrepreneurship audience

In 2021 I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even after lease and payroll.

I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting clients.

I thought working harder was the answer. More hours, more hustle, more grind.

Then COVID hit and a client asked if I could train him over Zoom.

I said yes reluctantly — figured it was better than nothing.

But something interesting happened.

He got better results training from his home office than he ever did in my gym.

No commute time. No waiting for equipment. No excuses about traffic or parking.

That's when I realized the gym wasn't the solution for busy executives — it was the problem.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online with customized home programs.

Sometimes the breakthrough comes from being forced to do things differently.

---

## Post 5: RESULTS/PROOF (BOF)
**Type:** Results/Proof Post | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Engaged with transformation content

Dan M., SaaS CEO:

**Before:** Burned out, 42 lbs over target, hadn't trained in 3 years

**Challenge:** Travels 2 weeks per month, works 60-hour weeks, tried every program

**After 6 months:** Lost 42 lbs, squats 2x bodyweight, stays on track regardless of travel

His words: "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

**The difference:** 

Dan didn't need more willpower. He needed a system designed for executives who travel constantly and work unpredictable hours.

The Performance Stack syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week.

Most programs fight your lifestyle. This one works with it.

---

## Post 6: CTA (BOF)
**Type:** CTA Post | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Viewed case studies

Ready to stop being The Corner Office Couch and become The Executive Athlete?

The Executive Athlete Program is for male executives age 40-55 who:
- Run their own companies and make $300K+
- Haven't prioritized health in 3-5 years
- Are tired of programs that don't account for 60-hour weeks
- Want to lose 30 lbs without missing business meetings

**This is NOT for you if:**
- You want a quick fix or magic pill
- You're not willing to follow a system for 6 months
- You think online coaching can't work
- You're looking for the cheapest option

If you're ready to invest in a system that treats your body transformation as seriously as your business success, apply at reedperformance.co/apply.

I only work with 12 clients at a time. Enrollment opens when existing clients graduate.

---

## Post 7: VALUE (TOF)
**Type:** Value Post | **Funnel Stage:** TOF | **Retargeting:** N

Travel nutrition that actually works for executives:

**Client dinner strategy:**
- Protein first, always (steak, fish, chicken)
- Vegetables second (ask for extra)
- Skip the bread basket and dessert
- Limit alcohol to 1-2 drinks max

**Hotel room backup:**
- Pack protein powder and shaker bottle
- Order room service: grilled protein + vegetables
- Use hotel gym if available, bodyweight in room if not
- Hydrate aggressively (travel dehydrates you)

**Airport/flight protocol:**
- Eat before you fly (airport food is garbage)
- Bring nuts, protein bars for emergencies
- Water, not alcohol or soda

The executive who plans for travel stays on track. The one who "wings it" gains 5 lbs per trip.

What's your biggest travel nutrition challenge?

---

## Post 8: BELIEF-SHIFTING (MOF)
**Type:** Belief-Shifting Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Viewed pricing content

You think you can't afford to invest in health coaching.

Here's the math:

**Cost of staying stuck:**
- ~$500/month wasted on unused Peloton memberships and canceled trainers
- Lost productivity from energy crashes by 3pm
- Healthcare costs from declining health markers
- Every month closer to the heart attack his father had at 58

**Cost of transformation:**
- $12,000 for 6 months
- That's what you spend on a single business trip
- ROI shows up in increased energy, better decisions, sustained performance

You wouldn't hesitate to spend this amount on equipment that would increase your business productivity by 20%.

Your health and energy affect every decision you make, every meeting you attend, and every strategy you execute.

The ROI is immediate and ongoing.

---

## Post 9: STORY (MOF)
**Type:** Story Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Business owner audience

I used to think more hours in the gym meant better results.

Train harder, longer, more often.

But I was watching successful executives fail with this approach.

They'd hire me, show up for 2 weeks, then disappear when work got busy.

I was blaming them for "lacking commitment."

Then I realized: I was designing programs for people who don't run companies.

These executives weren't lacking discipline. They were using the wrong system for their environment.

That's when I developed The Performance Stack — training, nutrition, and recovery that syncs with 60-hour work weeks instead of fighting them.

Same executives who "failed" before started getting results.

The problem wasn't their character. It was my system design.

Sometimes the issue isn't the person — it's the approach.

---

## Post 10: RESULTS/PROOF (BOF)
**Type:** Results/Proof Post | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Health/wellness audience

Rick P., private equity partner:

**Before:** Prediabetic A1C of 6.2, gaining weight despite trying multiple approaches

**The intervention:** 6-month Executive Athlete Program with nutrition overhaul

**Results:** A1C dropped to 5.4, lost 28 lbs

His doctor's words: "I couldn't believe the numbers."

**What made the difference:**

Rick didn't need another generic program. He needed a system that worked with his private equity schedule — client dinners, travel, unpredictable hours.

The Performance Stack gave him protocols for every situation: hotel gyms, business meals, jet lag recovery.

Your health markers don't lie. When the system fits your life, the results follow.

---

## Post 11: POLARIZING/FILTER (MOF)
**Type:** Polarizing Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Fitness content engagers

Unpopular opinion:

If you're not willing to invest in expert guidance for your health, you're not serious about transformation.

You hire consultants for business strategy.
You hire accountants for taxes.
You hire lawyers for legal issues.

But you think you can figure out executive fitness with YouTube videos and free apps?

Here's the truth: Information without implementation is worthless.

You don't need more information. You need accountability, expertise, and a system designed for your specific situation.

If you disagree and think you can DIY your way to transformation, that's fine.

This isn't for you.

But if you're ready to treat your health with the same seriousness you treat your business, let's talk.

---

## Post 12: VALUE (TOF)
**Type:** Value Post | **Funnel Stage:** TOF | **Retargeting:** N

Energy management for 12-hour workdays:

**Morning (6-9am):**
- 15-minute movement session before emails
- Protein-rich breakfast (eggs, Greek yogurt, protein shake)
- Hydrate immediately upon waking

**Midday (12-1pm):**
- Walk outside for 10 minutes (sunlight resets circadian rhythm)
- Lunch with protein and vegetables
- Avoid sugar crashes with balanced meals

**Evening (6-8pm):**
- Light movement or stretching
- Dinner 3 hours before bed
- Blue light blockers after sunset

**The principle:** Your energy is finite. Manage it like the valuable resource it is.

The executive who protects his energy outperforms the one who burns through it.

What's your biggest energy drain during the day?

---

## Post 13: BELIEF-SHIFTING (MOF)
**Type:** Belief-Shifting Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Time management content

You think taking time for health steals from your business productivity.

Here's what actually happens:

**The exhausted executive:**
- Mental fog by 3pm
- Poor decision-making when tired
- Irritable in meetings
- Misses opportunities due to low energy

**The Executive Athlete:**
- Sustained energy throughout 12-hour days
- Sharp thinking in afternoon meetings
- Calm under pressure
- Present and engaged with team

Time invested in your health is time invested in your performance everywhere else.

You already know this in business — you invest in technology upgrades, team training, and system improvements because they multiply your effectiveness.

Your health is the foundation that makes everything else possible.

---

## Post 14: CTA (BOF)
**Type:** CTA Post | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Viewed program details

The Executive Athlete Program opens enrollment when existing clients graduate.

Right now, I have 2 spots available.

**Investment:** $12,000 for 6 months (or 6 payments of $2,100)

**What you get:**
- Custom training programs for your home office
- Business-travel nutrition protocols
- Weekly video reviews with form corrections
- Biweekly 1-on-1 strategy calls
- Private Slack access for real-time support

**Guarantee:** If you don't lose 20 lbs in the first 90 days while following the protocol, I work with you free until you do.

This is for executives who are done making excuses and ready to become The Executive Athlete.

If that's you, apply at reedperformance.co/apply.

---

## Post 15: STORY (MOF)
**Type:** Story Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Transformation content

I was the fat kid nobody believed in.

Got picked last for teams. Avoided the pool. Hated looking in mirrors.

That shame drove me to fitness, but it also taught me something important:

The people who need transformation the most are often the ones who've given up on themselves.

That's why I work with executives who've tried everything and failed.

The successful CEO who's embarrassed by his reflection.
The private equity partner who's prediabetic.
The company owner who's following his father's path to a heart attack at 58.

They've built incredible businesses but feel like failures in their own bodies.

I help them prove their own critics wrong — including the critic in their head.

Because if you can build a million-dollar company, you can absolutely build the body you want.

You just need the right system.

---

## Post 16: VALUE (TOF)
**Type:** Value Post | **Funnel Stage:** TOF | **Retargeting:** N

Hotel gym workout that actually works:

**If they have weights:**
- Dumbbell squats: 3 sets of 12
- Dumbbell chest press: 3 sets of 10
- Dumbbell rows: 3 sets of 10
- Walking lunges: 3 sets of 20

**If it's just cardio equipment:**
- Treadmill intervals: 1 minute fast, 1 minute recovery, repeat 15 times
- Bodyweight circuit in your room after

**If the gym sucks:**
- Bodyweight in your room: push-ups, squats, planks, lunges
- 20 minutes total, done

**The principle:** Consistency beats perfection. The workout you do beats the perfect workout you skip.

Most executives use travel as an excuse. Smart executives use it as an opportunity to prove their system works anywhere.

What's your go-to travel workout?

---

## Post 17: BELIEF-SHIFTING (MOF)
**Type:** Belief-Shifting Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Self-improvement content

You think you can figure this out yourself.

Here's why that's costing you years:

**The DIY approach:**
- 3 years of trial and error
- Multiple failed programs
- Frustration and self-blame
- Still catching your reflection in elevator mirrors

**Expert guidance designed for executives:**
- 6 months to transformation
- System built for your specific challenges
- Accountability that works with your schedule
- Proven results with executives just like you

You wouldn't try to perform surgery on yourself just because you could research medical procedures online.

Complex transformations require expert guidance from someone who has successfully navigated the same challenges hundreds of times before.

The fastest path to results is learning from someone who has solved this exact problem for people in your exact situation.

---

## Post 18: RESULTS/PROOF (BOF)
**Type:** Results/Proof Post | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Success stories

The numbers don't lie:

52 out of 60 clients achieve their transformation when they follow The Performance Stack.

That's an 87% success rate.

**Compare that to:**
- Generic fitness programs: 13% stick with it past 90 days
- Gym memberships: 67% never used after January
- Personal trainers: Most executives cancel within ___

**The difference:** The Performance Stack is designed specifically for executives who work 60-hour weeks and travel constantly.

It's not about willpower. It's about system design.

When the system fits your lifestyle, success becomes predictable.

When it doesn't, failure is almost guaranteed.

---

## Post 19: POLARIZING/FILTER (MOF)
**Type:** Polarizing Post | **Funnel Stage:** MOF | **Retargeting:** Y - High-income audience

Controversial take:

If you make $300K+ and you're still making excuses about not having time for your health, you're lying to yourself.

You have time. You just don't prioritize it.

You'll spend 2 hours in a client dinner but won't spend 15 minutes on a workout.

You'll research business investments for weeks but won't invest in the foundation that makes everything else possible.

You'll hire consultants for every area of your business but think you can DIY your health transformation.

The truth: You don't have a time problem. You have a priority problem.

If you disagree, unfollow me now.

But if you're ready to stop making excuses and start making results, let's talk.

---

## Post 20: CTA (BOF)
**Type:** CTA Post | **Funnel Stage:** BOF | **Retargeting:** Y - Engaged with multiple posts

Question: Will you still be catching your reflection in that elevator mirror 6 months from now feeling embarrassed and exhausted?

Or will you be the executive who finally cracked the code on getting in the best shape of his life?

The choice is yours.

I only work with 12 active clients at a time because that's what allows me to give each executive the attention they deserve.

Enrollment only opens when an existing client graduates.

If you're ready to stop being The Corner Office Couch and become The Executive Athlete, go to reedperformance.co/apply right now.

I'll personally review every application and if you're a good fit, we'll schedule a call to discuss your specific situation.

Either way, thanks for following along.

The question is: What are you going to do about it?

fitness-sparse.content:
# Facebook/Instagram Ad Copy — Reed Performance

## Ad 1: Problem Agitation (Cold Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
You're running a $2M company but can't run a mile without wheezing. Something's gotta give.

**Primary Text (Long):**
You're running a $2M company but can't run a mile without wheezing.

Here's what I see with every executive who walks into my program:

You built something incredible. You manage teams, close deals, make decisions that affect hundreds of people. But somewhere along the way, you stopped taking care of the one thing that makes it all possible — your body.

Now you're 42, catching your reflection in the elevator mirror every morning, and thinking "How the hell did I get here?"

Your Peloton collects dust. You've hired three trainers this year and canceled on all of them. You downloaded MyFitnessPal twice and abandoned it after 2 weeks.

The problem isn't willpower. The problem is every fitness program was built for people who don't run companies.

I'm Marcus Reed. I help executives like you get in the best shape of your life without leaving your home office. No gym memberships. No 5am boot camps. Just a system that fits the reality of a 60-hour work week.

**Headline:** Stop avoiding the mirror

**Description:** Real fitness for real executives

**CTA Button:** Learn More

**Suggested Image/Video:** Split image — executive in suit looking tired/stressed vs. same type of person looking strong and confident

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Cold (business owners, executives, high-income demographics)
- Optimization: Landing page views
- Daily budget: $50-100

---

## Ad 2: Personal Story Hook (Cold Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
In 2021 I was training 12 hours a day and still barely breaking even. Here's what changed everything...

**Primary Text (Long):**
In 2021 I was training 12 hours a day at my gym and still barely breaking even after lease and payroll.

I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting the clients I was supposed to help. The irony wasn't lost on me — I was a personal trainer who was out of shape.

Then COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom. I said yes, figuring it was better than nothing.

That one session changed everything.

I realized I could help just as many people without a physical location. Without the overhead. Without the burnout.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online. Now I coach executives remotely with customized home programs.

Here's what I learned: The best results don't happen in a gym. They happen when you have a system that fits your actual life.

If you're an executive who's tried every fitness approach and nothing stuck, it's not because you lack discipline. It's because you've been using systems built for people who don't run companies.

I've helped 340 clients over 11 years. I know what works for busy leaders.

**Headline:** From burnt out to breakthrough

**Description:** My story + what I learned

**CTA Button:** Learn More

**Suggested Image/Video:** Before/after of Marcus or a professional photo of him in his home office setup

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Cold (entrepreneurs, business owners)
- Optimization: Landing page views
- Daily budget: $30-75

---

## Ad 3: Social Proof (Warm Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
"Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like." — Dan M., SaaS CEO who lost 42 lbs in 6 months

**Primary Text (Long):**
"Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

That's Dan M., a SaaS CEO who came to me burned out, 42 lbs over target, and hadn't trained in 3 years.

Six months later? He lost 42 lbs, squats 2x bodyweight, and travels 2 weeks per month while staying on track.

Or Rick P., a private equity partner who was prediabetic with an A1C of 6.2. After 6 months in my program, his A1C dropped to 5.4 and he lost 28 lbs. His doctor couldn't believe the numbers.

What's different about my approach?

I don't give you a cookie-cutter program. I build a system around your schedule, your travel, your 60-hour weeks.

The Executive Athlete Program isn't about finding more time. It's about making the time you have count.

52 out of my last 60 clients hit their target weight. Not because they had more willpower than you. Because they had a better system.

**Headline:** Real results, real executives

**Description:** See what's possible

**CTA Button:** Apply Now

**Suggested Image/Video:** Client transformation photos (with permission) or professional photo of Marcus

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Warm (website visitors, video viewers)
- Optimization: Purchases
- Daily budget: $___-150

---

## Ad 4: Belief-Shift Content (Retargeting)

**Primary Text (Short):**
Stop trying to fit your business schedule around fitness. Start fitting fitness around your business schedule.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Stop trying to fit your business schedule around fitness. Start fitting fitness around your business schedule.

Every executive I work with has been told the same lie: "You need to wake up at 5am and hit the gym before work."

Bullshit.

You know what happens when you try to force a gym routine into a CEO schedule? You miss workouts. You feel guilty. You quit.

Here's the truth: The most successful executives don't have more time. They have better systems.

You wouldn't run your business with a one-size-fits-all strategy. Why would you approach your health that way?

When a client travels 2 weeks per month, I don't tell him to find a gym in every city. I teach him how to use hotel rooms and bodyweight protocols.

When someone has back-to-back calls until 7pm, I don't schedule them for 6am workouts. I build 15-minute strength circuits they can do between meetings.

The goal isn't to become a fitness fanatic. The goal is to become the executive athlete — someone who treats their body like the asset it is.

Your company needs you healthy. Your family needs you energetic. Your legacy needs you around.

Stop leaving your health to chance.

**Headline:** [No headline needed for content ads]

**Description:** [No description needed]

**CTA Button:** [No CTA button — pure content]

**Suggested Image/Video:** Marcus in a home office setup demonstrating a quick exercise, or infographic showing "gym schedule vs executive schedule"

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Retargeting (engaged with previous ads)
- Optimization: Post engagement
- Daily budget: $25-50

---

## Ad 5: Direct Offer (Warm Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
Ready to become the executive athlete? The Executive Athlete Program enrollment is open for 12 spots.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Ready to become the executive athlete?

You know the guy I'm talking about. The high performer who takes his body as seriously as his P&L. Who walks into every room with the confidence that comes from being strong. Who has the energy to crush a 12-hour day and still play catch with his kids.

That's not some fantasy. That's what happens when you stop treating fitness like a hobby and start treating it like a business system.

The Executive Athlete Program is a 6-month transformation designed specifically for executives who run companies making $500K to $5M.

Here's what you get:
• Custom training program that works in your home office
• Nutrition protocol that accounts for business travel
• Weekly video check-ins and biweekly 1-on-1 calls
• Private Slack access for real-time support
• The Performance Stack — my 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to a 60-hour work week

Investment: $12,000 for 6 months (or 6 monthly payments of $2,100)

Guarantee: If you don't lose 20 lbs in the first 90 days while following the protocol, I work with you free until you do.

This is for executives who are done making excuses and ready to become the leader their company and family deserve.

If that's you, apply now. I only work with 12 clients at a time.
