relationship-dense.content:
# Facebook Content Playbook
## Second Act Dating — David Alvarez

---

## POST 1 — VALUE POST (TOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Top of Funnel (Awareness)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target divorced men 45-60

**The women you attract are a mirror of your emotional state.**

If you keep attracting unavailable women, women who remind you of your ex, or women with serious baggage — look in the mirror.

Here's what's actually happening: You're dating from the wound of your divorce instead of from wholeness. Wounded energy attracts wounded energy. It's that simple.

Most divorced men think they need better matches. What they actually need are better attachment patterns.

When you heal your patterns and show up from emotional intelligence instead of reactivity, you naturally attract women who are doing their own work.

Try this today: Before your next date, ask yourself "Am I dating to find a partner or to prove my ex wrong?" 

Your answer will tell you everything about why your results haven't changed.

What patterns have you noticed in the women you attract?

---

## POST 2 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST (MOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Middle of Funnel (Belief Shift)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men researching dating coaches

**You think you need better matches — you actually need better attachment patterns.**

Here's the brutal truth: The problem isn't the dating pool. It's what you're putting out there as bait.

A fisherman who keeps catching the wrong fish doesn't blame the lake — he changes his bait.

If you keep attracting the same type of woman over and over, the common denominator isn't them. It's you.

Dr. Amir Levine's research in "Attached" proves this: Anxiously attached people attract avoidantly attached partners, creating the same dysfunctional patterns that destroyed your marriage.

Secure attachment attracts secure attachment.

When you heal your attachment wounds and show up from wholeness instead of from the pain of your divorce, everything changes.

The quality of women who notice you. The depth of conversations you have. The ease with which real connection happens.

Stop blaming the apps. Start examining your patterns.

Have you noticed yourself attracting the same type of woman repeatedly?

---

## POST 3 — STORY POST (MOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Middle of Funnel (Connection)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target divorced men considering coaching

**I was 38, making $180K, and sleeping on a mattress on the floor.**

After my divorce in 2014, I spent 18 months dating badly. I was angry, distrustful, and kept attracting women who reminded me of my ex-wife.

Despite my success as a therapist, I couldn't figure out why every relationship felt like a repeat of my marriage.

Then my mentor said something that broke me open:

"David, you're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails."

He was right. I was dating from the wound, not from wholeness.

I did 2 years of my own therapy, closed my private practice, and developed what became The Second Act Method.

Today I have a 3000-person community of men who've made the same journey. I remarried in 2021 to a woman I met in my own program community.

The difference? I learned to date from emotional intelligence instead of from anger.

If you're dating to prove someone wrong, you'll attract someone wrong.

What's driving your dating — healing or proving?

---

## POST 4 — VALUE POST (TOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Top of Funnel (Awareness)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men struggling with dating apps

**Chemistry often means familiar dysfunction. Compatibility means shared values.**

Most divorced men get this backwards. They chase the spark and ignore the substance.

Here's what I've learned after coaching 420 men: That instant chemistry you feel? It's often your nervous system recognizing familiar patterns — including familiar problems.

Real compatibility is quieter. It's shared values, similar life goals, and complementary attachment styles.

The woman who gives you butterflies might be triggering your anxious attachment. The woman who feels "easy" to be around might be your actual match.

Try this framework on your next date:
- Chemistry check: Am I attracted to her?
- Compatibility check: Do our values align?
- Attachment check: Does she feel safe or familiar?

Safe and attracted beats familiar and excited every time.

Your future wife might not give you butterflies on date one. But she'll give you peace for the next ___.

What's been your experience — chemistry or compatibility?

---

## POST 5 — CTA POST (BOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Bottom of Funnel (Direct Invitation)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men who've engaged with content

**There are two types of divorced men over 45:**

The Wound-Dating Dad who keeps attracting women who remind him of his ex, burns $400-600 monthly on dating apps that don't work, and feels invisible to quality women.

And The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man who attracts the right women because he finally knows who he is, dates from wholeness instead of wounds, and feels confident in his ability to recognize healthy love.

The difference? The Second Act Method — a 3-phase Gottman-based protocol for rebuilding attachment capacity after divorce.

This is for you if:
- You're 45-60, divorced, and tired of dating the same type of woman over and over
- You make $200K+ but feel invisible to quality women
- You're ready to do the inner work that most divorced guys avoid

This is NOT for you if:
- You think the problem is just your photos
- You're looking for pickup artist tactics
- You're not ready to examine your own patterns

If you're ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man, DM me "SECOND ACT" and I'll send you details about the program.

---

## POST 6 — RESULTS/PROOF POST (BOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Bottom of Funnel (Social Proof)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men considering investment

**"The only reason I'm not alone at 52 is this program."**

Dr. James K., cardiac surgeon. Divorced 3 years. Zero second dates in 18 months despite premium app subscriptions and individual therapy.

He was successful in every area except the one that mattered most — finding someone to share his life with.

Here's what changed:

Instead of optimizing his dating profile for the hundredth time, he learned The Second Act Method. He did the emotional work to heal from his divorce. He rebuilt his attachment capacity using Gottman-based protocols.

Result: Engaged to a woman he met at our November retreat. 8 months from invisible to chosen.

The difference wasn't his photos or his bio. It was showing up from wholeness instead of from the wound of his divorce.

James didn't need more matches. He needed better emotional intelligence.

When you heal your patterns, you attract women who've healed theirs.

Out of our last 80 clients, 63 achieved meaningful relationship success using this same approach.

What would finding your person be worth to you?

---

## POST 7 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST (MOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Middle of Funnel (Belief Shift)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men making excuses about timing

**"I'll start dating seriously when work slows down."**

Here's the problem with that logic: Work never slows down. There's always another project, another deadline, another reason to postpone.

You're using career success as armor against vulnerability.

Harvard's Grant Study — the longest-running study on happiness — found that good relationships keep us happier and healthier than career success or wealth.

The study's director concluded: "Relationship quality is the strongest predictor of life satisfaction."

You already know this. Your first marriage probably failed because you prioritized work over connection.

The right relationship doesn't compete with your career — it enhances it. My wife makes me better at business, not worse.

But here's what really happens when you wait: While you're preparing to be ready, other men are taking action. Your ideal partner meets someone else on Tuesday.

You don't need perfect timing. You need intentional action.

The Second Act Method fits into your executive schedule because finding your person is non-negotiable.

What's really stopping you — time or fear?

---

## POST 8 — VALUE POST (TOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Top of Funnel (Awareness)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men frustrated with dating apps

**You don't have a dating app problem. You have an attachment wound problem.**

Bumble and Hinge aren't broken. Your approach is.

Here's what's actually happening: You're swiping from desperation instead of intention. You're messaging from scarcity instead of abundance. You're dating from the wound of your divorce instead of from wholeness.

No app optimization can fix that energy.

Dr. John Gottman's 40+ years of research proves that emotional intelligence and secure attachment are the strongest predictors of relationship success.

Surface-level attraction fades. Emotional attunement creates lasting love.

Try this reframe: Instead of asking "How do I get more matches?" ask "How do I become the man my ideal partner would choose?"

The answer isn't better photos. It's better emotional intelligence.

When you show up from wholeness, quality women notice — regardless of the platform.

What would change if you focused on healing instead of swiping?

---

## POST 9 — STORY POST (MOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Middle of Funnel (Connection)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men relating to transformation stories

**Carlos M. was drinking too much and dating women 15 years younger.**

Restaurant owner. Divorced 2 years. Running from himself and recreating the same patterns that destroyed his marriage.

He thought the problem was finding "better quality women." What he actually needed was to become a better quality man.

Through The Second Act Method, Carlos did the work most divorced men avoid:
- He got sober
- He healed his attachment wounds  
- He learned to date age-appropriate women
- He stopped running from vulnerability

Result: 9 months in a healthy relationship with someone who actually fits his life.

As Carlos told me: "I stopped running from myself."

That's the transformation. Not just finding someone — becoming someone worth finding.

Carlos didn't need different women. He needed to become a different man.

The Second Act Method doesn't just change your dating results. It changes your identity.

From The Wound-Dating Dad to The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man.

What would you need to stop running from?

---

## POST 10 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST (MOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Middle of Funnel (Belief Shift)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men hesitating about investment

**If you hesitate to invest $9,500 in finding love, you're teaching women to hesitate about you.**

Your relationship with money reveals your relationship with commitment.

Men who won't bet on themselves attract women who won't bet on them either.

Here's the math: You're already burning roughly $400-600 monthly on dating apps, premium subscriptions, and therapy. That's over $___annually with zero relationship success.

The Second Act Method costs $9,500 for 6 months and ends the expensive cycle of failed dating permanently.

But this isn't about money. It's about mindset.

High-value men understand that quality costs more. They invest in solutions, not Band-Aids.

Quality women notice this energy immediately. They're attracted to men who make decisive investments in their own growth.

Cheap investments create cheap outcomes. Premium investment in yourself attracts premium partners.

Your financial standards set your relationship standards.

What message are you sending about your commitment level?

---

## POST 11 — POLARIZING/FILTER POST (MOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Middle of Funnel (Filtering)  
**Retargeting:** N — Organic reach only

**Dating coaches are NOT for 25-year-olds who don't know how to talk to women.**

If you think all dating advice is pickup artist garbage, you're right about 90% of it.

But post-divorce dating at 45+ requires specialized expertise. You're not learning how to get phone numbers at bars — you're learning how to rebuild attachment capacity after trauma.

That's why I studied under Dr. John Gottman at his institute from 2016-2018. This isn't generic dating advice. It's relationship science applied to your specific situation.

Most dating coaches have never been married, never been divorced, and never helped a man rebuild after his life exploded.

I've coached 420 men through this exact journey. I've been divorced, done the work, and remarried successfully.

If you think you can figure this out with YouTube videos and Reddit forums, go ahead. Most men try that for 2-4 years before getting serious about expert guidance.

If you want to accelerate your results and learn from someone who's walked this path, we should talk.

The Second Act Method isn't for everyone. It's for men who are done wasting time.

Are you ready to get serious about finding love?

---

## POST 12 — VALUE POST (TOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Top of Funnel (Awareness)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men looking for practical advice

**How to spot red flags in the first 10 minutes of a date:**

She talks only about her ex-husband (anxious attachment).

She seems too good to be true and love-bombs you (avoidant attachment).

She's defensive about basic questions about her life (unhealed trauma).

She checks her phone constantly (not present or interested).

She makes everything about money or what you can provide (transactional mindset).

Green flags to look for instead:

She asks thoughtful questions about your life and actually listens.

She's curious about your interests without trying to change them.

She mentions friends and family in positive ways (healthy relationships).

She's comfortable with appropriate silence (secure attachment).

She expresses appreciation for the date without overdoing it.

The Second Act Method teaches you Gottman-based assessment skills so you can recognize secure attachment patterns quickly.

Most divorced men waste months on women who show red flags on date one.

Your time is valuable. Learn to protect it.

What red flags have you learned to watch for?

---

## POST 13 — RESULTS/PROOF POST (BOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Bottom of Funnel (Social Proof)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men considering program

**Mike D. avoided dating entirely for 18 months because he was "too busy building his company."**

Tech founder. Used work as armor against vulnerability. Terrified of getting hurt again.

He tried to figure it out himself through self-help books and isolation. Spent 18 months "working on himself" while actually avoiding the work that mattered.

Through The Second Act Method plus 2 retreats, Mike learned that healing happens in relationship, not in isolation.

Result: In a committed relationship with someone he calls "the right person at the right time." 11 months from avoidance to engagement.

As Mike told me: "I wasn't broken. I was healing."

That's the insight. You're not broken. You don't need to be perfect before you start dating. You need the right framework to date while healing.

The Second Act Method gives you that framework.

Out of our last 80 clients, 63 achieved meaningful relationship success by learning to heal in community instead of in isolation.

Within the first 30 days, most men report 2-3 meaningful first dates and a noticeable shift in how they show up emotionally.

What would change if you stopped trying to figure it out alone?

---

## POST 14 — CTA POST (BOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Bottom of Funnel (Direct Invitation)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men who've watched video content

**The Second Act Method: Investment breakdown**

$9,500 for 6 months breaks down to $___per month.

You're already burning roughly $400-600 monthly on dating apps, premium subscriptions, and therapy that don't address the root cause.

Here's what you get instead:
- Weekly group coaching calls with expert guidance
- Monthly 1-on-1 sessions for personal challenges
- Access to the 3000-person Brotherhood community
- Quarterly weekend retreats for real-world practice
- Profile and text review from someone who understands post-divorce dating
- Gottman-based framework library for relationship building

Plus: 12-month extension if you don't have 3 meaningful dates per month by month 4.

This is for divorced men 45-60 who make $200K+ and are ready to do the inner work that most guys avoid.

This is NOT for men looking for quick fixes or pickup artist tactics.

The program caps at 40 active members. Doors open once per quarter aligned with our retreat schedule.

If you're ready to stop burning money on approaches that don't work and invest in a system that does, DM me "INVESTMENT" for details.

---

## POST 15 — IDENTITY POST (MOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Middle of Funnel (Identity Shift)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men considering transformation

**You're either becoming The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man or staying The Wound-Dating Dad.**

The Wound-Dating Dad:
- Dates from the wound of his divorce
- Attracts women who remind him of his ex
- Burns $400-600 monthly on apps that don't work
- Feels invisible to quality women
- Suspicious of every woman's motives
- Wakes up alone at 6am in an empty bed

The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man:
- Dates from wholeness, not wounds
- Attracts women who've done their own work
- Selective about who gets his time and energy
- Confident in his ability to recognize healthy love
- At peace with his history, excited about his future
- Wakes up next to someone who chose him

The difference? The Second Act Method — a 3-phase Gottman-based protocol for rebuilding attachment capacity after divorce.

Phase 1: Grounding (heal the wound)
Phase 2: Rebuilding (establish new patterns)  
Phase 3: Attracting (practice with right-fit women)

Your kids are watching. Show them what healthy love looks like.

Which man are you becoming?

---

## POST 16 — VALUE POST (TOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Top of Funnel (Awareness)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men struggling with confidence

**Emotional intelligence is the ultimate aphrodisiac for quality women.**

Forget pickup lines. Forget "game." Forget trying to be someone you're not.

Quality women at 40+ have been through enough relationships to spot authentic emotional intelligence immediately.

They're attracted to men who:
- Can regulate their emotions under pressure
- Listen without trying to fix everything
- Express vulnerability appropriately
- Handle conflict without attacking or withdrawing
- Show up consistently, not just when it's convenient

This is what Dr. John Gottman's research proves: Emotional attunement creates lasting attraction. Surface-level charm fades.

The Second Act Method teaches you these skills through Gottman-based protocols, not manipulation tactics.

When you show up from emotional intelligence instead of emotional reactivity, everything changes:
- The quality of women who notice you
- The depth of conversations you have
- The ease with which real connection happens

You don't need to be perfect. You need to be present.

What would change if you focused on emotional growth instead of dating tactics?

---

## POST 17 — STORY POST (MOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Middle of Funnel (Connection)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men relating to mentor moments

**"You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong."**

My mentor's words hit like a sledgehammer.

I was 38, successful therapist, making $180K. But I was sleeping on a mattress on the floor and dating badly for 18 months after my divorce.

Every woman I met either reminded me of my ex or triggered my anger about the divorce.

I thought I was looking for love. What I was actually doing was trying to prove that I wasn't the problem in my marriage.

That realization broke me open.

I spent the next 2 years doing my own therapy. Not just processing the divorce, but learning how to date from wholeness instead of from wounds.

I closed my private practice and developed what became The Second Act Method.

Today I have a 3000-person community and I'm remarried to a woman I met in my own program community.

The difference? I learned to date for the right reasons.

If you're dating to prove someone wrong, you'll attract someone wrong.

If you're dating to find someone right, you'll become someone worth finding.

What's really driving your dating?

---

## POST 18 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST (MOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Middle of Funnel (Belief Shift)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men avoiding group programs

**"Group programs won't work for me because my situation is unique."**

Every divorced man thinks this. Your shame makes you believe you're the only one who feels invisible to quality women or attracts the wrong type.

Here's the truth: Your situation isn't unique. Your pain makes you think it is.

I've coached 420 men. The patterns are remarkably similar:
- Dating from the wound instead of wholeness
- Attracting women who remind them of their ex
- Confusing chemistry with compatibility
- Using work to avoid vulnerability
- Feeling invisible despite success in other areas

Group coaching works because shame dies in community, not isolation.

When you realize other successful men have walked your exact path, everything shifts. You stop feeling broken and start feeling normal.

The Brotherhood community has 3000 men who understand your journey. They've been where you are. They're where you want to be.

What takes 3 years alone takes 6 months with expert guidance and peer support.

Malcolm Gladwell's research proves this: Expertise requires mentorship and community, not just individual effort.

What would change if you stopped trying to heal in isolation?

---

## POST 19 — RESULTS/PROOF POST (BOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Bottom of Funnel (Social Proof)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men wanting proof of results

**The numbers: 63 out of 80 clients achieved meaningful relationship success.**

That's a 78.75% success rate using The Second Act Method.

Here's what "meaningful relationship success" means:
- 3+ meaningful dates per month with emotionally healthy women
- Clear ability to recognize and avoid red flags
- Dating from wholeness instead of from wounds
- Either in a committed relationship or confidently selective

Within the first 30 days, most men report:
- 2-3 meaningful first dates (not just any dates)
- A noticeable shift in how they show up emotionally
- Increased confidence in their ability to recognize healthy women
- Decreased reactivity to triggers from their divorce

The 17 men who didn't achieve these results? They didn't implement the system. They wanted results without doing the emotional work.

The Second Act Method works if you work it.

It's a 3-phase Gottman-based protocol for rebuilding attachment capacity after divorce. Not magic. Not manipulation. Science.

This is for men who are ready to do the inner work that most divorced guys avoid.

What would a 78% success rate be worth to you?

---

## POST 20 — CTA POST (BOF)
**Funnel Stage:** Bottom of Funnel (Direct Invitation)  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men ready to take action

**Ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man?**

Here's what that looks like:

You wake up next to someone who chose you — not settled for you.

You're selective about who gets your time because you finally know your worth.

When you walk into a room, you carry yourself like a man at peace with his story, and the right women notice.

Your kids see you in a healthy relationship.

You're no longer dating from the wound of your divorce but from the wholeness of who you've become.

The Second Act Method makes this transformation possible in 6 months instead of 3 years of trial and error.

This is for you if:
- You're 45-60, divorced, and make $200K+
- You're tired of dating the same type of woman over and over
- You're ready to do the inner work that creates lasting change

This is NOT for you if:
- You think the problem is just your photos
- You want quick fixes without emotional work
- You're not ready to invest in your transformation

The program caps at 40 active members. Next cohort starts with our quarterly retreat.

If you're ready to stop dating from wounds and start attracting from wholeness, DM me "READY" and I'll send you the details.

Your person is waiting for the man you're becoming.

# Facebook/Instagram Ad Copy — Second Act Dating

---

## **Ad 1: Problem/Agitation (Cold Audience)**

**Primary Text (Short):**
You're 48, make $350K, and still can't get a second date. The apps aren't the problem.

**Primary Text (Long):**
You're 48, make $350K, and still can't get a second date. The apps aren't the problem.

Here's what's actually happening: You're dating from the wound of your divorce, not from wholeness. Every swipe, every message, every first date — you're unconsciously trying to prove your ex wrong instead of finding the right woman.

That's why you keep attracting women who remind you of what you're trying to escape.

I know because I was there. After my divorce in 2014, I spent 18 months dating badly. I was angry, distrustful, and sleeping on a mattress on the floor despite making $180K in my therapy practice.

My mentor finally told me: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails."

It broke me open. And it changed everything.

Today, I've helped 420 divorced men over 45 find real partnerships — not just matches, but women who choose them without settling.

The Second-Act Dating Audit shows you exactly where you're dating from the wound vs. dating from wholeness. Takes 3 minutes. Changes everything.

**Headline:** Stop Dating From Your Divorce

**Description:** Free audit reveals the truth

**CTA Button:** Learn More

**Suggested Image/Video:** Split-screen image: frustrated man on phone/dating app vs. confident man having dinner with a woman (both age-appropriate, professional looking)

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Cold (interests: divorce recovery, dating after 40, relationship coaching)
- Optimization: Landing page views
- Budget: $___-100/day

---

## **Ad 2: Story/Transformation (Cold Audience)**

**Primary Text (Short):**
"You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong." This one sentence changed my life.

**Primary Text (Long):**
"You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong."

My mentor said this to me in 2015. I was 38, recently divorced, making $180K in my therapy practice, and sleeping on a mattress on the floor.

I'd been dating for 18 months with zero success. Every woman reminded me of my ex. Every date felt like an interrogation. I was angry, suspicious, and completely lost.

That one sentence broke me open.

I realized I wasn't looking for love — I was looking for vindication. I was trying to prove I was worthy of what my ex couldn't give me. No wonder every relationship failed.

I spent the next 2 years doing my own work. Real work. Gottman Method attachment theory. Understanding how divorce wounds show up in dating patterns.

Today, I'm remarried to an incredible woman I met through my own program community. And I've helped 420 divorced men over 45 find the same thing.

Not just dates. Not just matches. Real partnerships with women who choose them, not settle for them.

The Second-Act Dating Audit shows you if you're dating from your wound or from your wholeness. Most men are shocked by what they discover.

**Headline:** From Divorced & Angry to Remarried

**Description:** My mentor's brutal truth

**CTA Button:** Learn More

**Suggested Image/Video:** Before/after style image of David (professional headshot progression) or video testimonial format with David speaking directly to camera

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Cold (lookalike of email list, interests in relationship coaching)
- Optimization: Landing page views
- Budget: $___-150/day

---

## **Ad 3: Social Proof/Results (Warm Audience)**

**Primary Text (Short):**
Dr. James K. was divorced 3 years with 0 second dates in 18 months. Now he's engaged.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Dr. James K. was divorced 3 years with 0 second dates in 18 months. Now he's engaged.

"The only reason I'm not alone at 52 is this program." — Dr. James K., cardiac surgeon

James had everything on paper. Successful practice. Great income. But he couldn't get past a first date to save his life.

The problem wasn't his profile. It wasn't the apps. It wasn't even the women.

James was dating from the wound of his divorce. Every conversation felt like a job interview. Every date felt like he was auditioning for approval.

Sound familiar?

Through The Second Act Method, James learned to date from wholeness instead of wound. He stopped trying to prove he was worthy and started choosing women who already saw his worth.

8 months later, he got engaged to a woman he met at our November retreat.

James isn't special. He's one of 420 men I've helped find real partnerships after divorce.

The difference? He stopped dating to prove his ex wrong and started dating to find his person.

The Second-Act Dating Audit shows you if you're dating from wound or wholeness. Most men are shocked by what they discover.

**Headline:** 0 Second Dates to Engaged

**Description:** How James changed everything

**CTA Button:** Learn More

**Suggested Image/Video:** Professional photo of a confident man (age-appropriate) or graphic showing "0 second dates → Engaged in 8 months"

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Warm (website visitors, video viewers)
- Optimization: Purchases
- Budget: $30-75/day

---

## **Ad 4: Retargeting/Belief-Shift (No Hard CTA)**

**Primary Text (Short):**
Your dating apps aren't broken. Your dating frame is.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Your dating apps aren't broken. Your dating frame is.

You think you need better matches. More attractive women. Younger women. Women who actually respond.

But here's what's really happening: You're operating from a scarcity frame that was created by your divorce.

Every swipe says "please pick me."
Every message says "I hope I'm good enough."
Every first date says "please don't leave like she did."

You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to heal a wound.

And wounded energy repels the exact women you actually want to attract.

The women who choose you from this frame? They're choosing you because you're safe, not because you're irresistible.

The women who reject you? They're rejecting your energy, not your profile.

I spent 18 months dating from this exact wound after my divorce. Angry, suspicious, desperate for validation. Every woman reminded me of my ex because that's the energy I was putting out.

When I shifted from dating for validation to dating for partnership, everything changed.

Same apps. Same city. Completely different results.

The frame you date from determines the women you attract.

**Headline:** It's Not Your Profile

**Description:** It's your frame

**CTA Button:** None (engagement-optimized post)

**Suggested Image/Video:** Simple text overlay on dark background: "Your dating apps aren't broken. Your dating frame is."

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Retargeting (engaged with previous ads/content)
- Optimization: Post engagement
- Budget: $___-50/day

---

## **Ad 5: Direct Offer (Warm Audience)**

**Primary Text (Short):**
Ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man? Applications now open.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man?

A guy who attracts the right women because he finally knows who he is. Who dates from wholeness, not from wound. Who gets chosen, not settled for.

If you're a divorced man over 45 who's tired of dating apps that lead nowhere and first dates that feel like job interviews, The Second Act Method is for you.

This isn't therapy. This isn't pickup artistry. This is a 6-month Gottman-based protocol for rebuilding your attachment capacity after divorce.

3 phases:
→ Grounding (4 weeks of post-divorce identity work)
→ Rebuilding (8 weeks of attachment pattern work)  
→ Attracting (12 weeks of real-world dating practice)

You'll get weekly group coaching calls, monthly 1-on-1s, profile audits, text review, and access to our 3000-person Brotherhood community.

The investment: $9500 for 6 months (or 3 payments of $3300).

This is for successful men who are serious about finding their person, not just their next date. If you make $200K+ and you're ready to date from your strength instead of your wound, apply now.

Cohorts cap at 40 active members. Doors open once per quarter.

**Headline:** The Second Act Method

**Description:** Applications now open

**CTA Button:** Apply Now

**Suggested Image/Video:** Professional photo of David or graphic showing the 3-phase program structure

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Warm only (previous engagers, website visitors)
- Optimization: Purchases
- Budget: $___-100/day

relationship-dense.content:
# YOUTUBE VIDEO SCRIPT
## Second Act Dating — David Alvarez

---

**VIDEO TITLE:** "Why Divorced Men Over 45 Keep Dating the Same Type of Woman (And How to Break the Pattern)"

**THUMBNAIL TEXT:** "STOP DATING YOUR EX"

**CORE BELIEF SHIFT:** From "I need better matches — the problem is the quality of women on apps" to "I'm dating from the wound of my divorce instead of from wholeness"

**DESCRIPTION:** Divorced men over 45: If you keep attracting women who remind you of your ex-wife, this video reveals why it's happening and how to break the pattern. David Alvarez, who's coached 420 men through post-divorce dating, explains the real reason you're invisible to quality women and shares the science-backed method that gets results. Get the free Second-Act Dating Audit: https://secondactdating.com/audit

**ESTIMATED RUNTIME:** 18-20 minutes

---

## 1. HOOK (0:00-0:30)
**[Word Count: 75]**

How to stop attracting women who remind you of your ex-wife without settling for someone you're not attracted to, so you can find a partner who chooses you and feel confident in your ability to recognize healthy love.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Why You Keep Dating Your Ex"]

Listen, if you're a divorced man over 45 and every woman you meet either ghosts you after one date or triggers the same emotional reactions your ex-wife did, you're not broken. You're not too old. And you're definitely not destined to die alone.

You're just dating from the wound instead of from wholeness.

---

## 2. INTRO (0:30-1:30)
**[Word Count: 150]**

My name is David Alvarez. I've coached 420 men through post-divorce dating over the last 9 years. I built and sold a dating site for $1.2M, and I remarried in 2021 to a woman I met in my own program community.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "David Alvarez - 420 Men Coached"]

In this video, you're going to learn three things. First, why you keep attracting the same type of woman over and over — and it's not what you think. Second, the real reason quality women seem invisible to you. And third, the exact framework I use with clients to break these patterns in 30 days or less.

Now, this is for divorced men age 45-60 who make good money but feel completely invisible in the dating market. If you're looking for pickup artist tactics or ways to date women half your age, this isn't for you.

If you're serious about finding real love the second time around, hit subscribe. I post weekly content for men who refuse to settle.

---

## 3. PROBLEM (1:30-4:00)
**[Word Count: 375]**

Here's what's really happening. You probably think your problem is the dating apps. You think you need better photos, a cleverer bio, maybe access to a higher-quality dating pool. You're burning $400 to $600 a month on premium subscriptions, dating coaches, and apps, thinking if you just optimize your approach, you'll finally get better matches.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "$400-600/month burned on apps"]

But here's the brutal truth: You're 47, successful, and you haven't had a second date in eight months. Every woman on Bumble either ghosts you or reminds you of your ex-wife. You're starting to wonder if there's something fundamentally wrong with you that everyone can see but no one will tell you.

You feel invisible to the women you actually want. Angry that your ex moved on first. Defeated after another promising connection fizzles out. And deep down, you're terrified that your kids see you as a broken man who can't figure out something as basic as finding a partner.

[PAUSE FOR EMPHASIS]

The consequences of staying stuck in this belief? You waste another two to four years dating the wrong people. You miss your window for finding lasting love. You become that bitter divorced guy who blames women and dating apps for everything. Your kids never see you in a healthy relationship.

And here's the thing that'll really mess with your head — your ex-wife isn't struggling like this. She's probably in a relationship already, posting photos with her new partner, while you're swiping through the same recycled profiles every night.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Your ex moved on... why haven't you?"]

You know what the real problem is? It's not the dating apps. It's not your age. It's not that all the good women are taken.

The real problem is that you're dating from the wound of your divorce instead of from wholeness.

---

## 4. NEW BELIEF / PARADIGM SHIFT (4:00-8:00)
**[Word Count: 600]**

Here's what I need you to understand: You attract what you are, not what you want. If you're dating from anger, you'll attract angry women. If you're dating from fear, you'll attract fearful women. If you're dating to prove your ex wrong, you'll attract women who are also trying to prove something.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "You attract what you are"]

**[PROOF BLOCK: Authority Reference]**
Dr. Amir Levine, in his book "Attached," explains that our attachment style determines who we're drawn to and who's drawn to us. Anxiously attached people attract avoidantly attached partners, creating the same dysfunctional patterns over and over. Secure attachment attracts secure attachment.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Universal Analogy]**
Think about it like this: A fisherman who keeps catching the wrong fish doesn't blame the lake — he changes his bait. If you keep attracting the same type of woman, the common denominator isn't the dating pool. It's what you're putting out there as bait.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Personal Story]**
After my divorce in 2014, I spent 18 months dating badly. I was angry, distrustful, and kept attracting women who reminded me of my ex. I was 38 years old, making $180K in a private therapy practice, and still sleeping on a mattress on the floor of a rental.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Dating from the wound"]

I tried everything — better photos, premium apps, dating coaches who taught me "game." Nothing worked because I was dating from the wound of my divorce, not from wholeness.

Then my mentor told me something that broke me open: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails."

**[PROOF BLOCK: Logic Trap]**
Here's the logic trap: If better dating app optimization was the real solution, then every man with professional photos and a clever bio would be in a happy relationship. But we both know that's not true. The guys with the slickest profiles often have the worst relationship outcomes because they're optimizing for the wrong thing.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Client Story]**
Dr. James K., a cardiac surgeon, came to me after being divorced three years with zero second dates in 18 months. He had premium subscriptions to every app, professional photos, even hired a dating coach. But he kept attracting women who were emotionally unavailable, just like his ex-wife.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Dr. James K. - 0 second dates in 18 months"]

Within six months of learning to date from wholeness instead of wounds, he got engaged to a woman he met at one of our retreats. As he told me: "The only reason I'm not alone at 52 is this program."

**[PROOF BLOCK: Cross-Domain Reference]**
You already know this works in business. You don't just hire based on resumes — you look for cultural fit, emotional intelligence, and long-term potential. You understand that the best business relationships are built on trust and mutual respect, not just surface-level qualifications. Dating is the same.

**[PROOF BLOCK: Reframe]**
What if I told you that feeling invisible to quality women isn't a bug — it's a feature? When you're dating from wounds, emotionally healthy women can sense it immediately. They're not rejecting you personally; they're protecting themselves from your unhealed trauma. That's actually their emotional intelligence working correctly.

---

## 5. PROOF / STORIES (8:00-12:00)
**[Word Count: 600]**

Let me tell you exactly how I discovered this. After my divorce, I was doing everything the dating experts told me to do. I had professional photos. I was messaging 30 women a week. I was going on two first dates a month that never went anywhere.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "30 messages/week, 2 dates/month"]

I was successful in every other area of my life, but I felt like a complete failure when it came to dating. I started thinking maybe I was just too damaged, too old, too set in my ways.

Then I had coffee with my mentor — a guy who'd been through his own divorce and found love again. I was complaining about how broken the dating apps were, how all the women were damaged, how nobody wanted a real relationship anymore.

He listened for about ten minutes, then he said: "David, you're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails."

[PAUSE]

It broke me open because it was true. I wasn't looking for love — I was looking for validation. I wasn't trying to find the right woman — I was trying to prove I wasn't the problem in my marriage.

That conversation led me to do two years of my own therapy. Real therapy, not just venting about my ex. I had to face the fact that I was carrying wounds from my divorce that were affecting every interaction I had with women.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "2 years of healing work"]

Here's what changed: When I started dating from wholeness instead of wounds, I attracted completely different women. Women who were also doing their own work. Women who weren't trying to fix me or be fixed by me. Women who chose me because of who I was, not because they needed something from me.

That's how I met my wife in 2021. She was in my program community, working on her own post-divorce healing. We connected because we were both whole people choosing each other, not wounded people trying to complete each other.

**Client Story: Carlos M.**

Carlos came to me as a restaurant owner who'd been divorced two years. He was drinking too much, dating women 15 years younger, and basically running from himself. He blamed the "dating scene" for his failures.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Carlos M. - Running from himself"]

Through the program, he realized he wasn't actually looking for a partner — he was looking for someone to distract him from his pain. Once he did the work to heal from his divorce, he naturally stopped being attracted to women who were also running from their problems.

He's been sober for over a year now and dating an age-appropriate partner for nine months. As he told me: "I stopped running from myself."

The pattern is always the same: heal the wound, change the attraction.

---

## 6. ACTION STEPS (12:00-15:00)
**[Word Count: 450]**

Alright, here's what you can do today to start breaking these patterns. I call this The Second Act Method, and it's based on Dr. John Gottman's research on what makes relationships actually work.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "The Second Act Method"]

**Step 1: Identify Your Attachment Wound**

Right now, I want you to think about the last three women you were attracted to. Write down their names. Now write down three characteristics they all shared. I guarantee you'll see a pattern — and that pattern probably mirrors something from your marriage that didn't work.

This isn't about blaming yourself or your ex. It's about recognizing that unhealed wounds create predictable attraction patterns. Awareness is the first step to change.

**Step 2: Stop Dating to Prove Your Ex Wrong**

Ask yourself this question: "Am I dating to find the right partner, or am I dating to prove I'm not the problem?" Be honest. If you're still angry at your ex, if you're still trying to show her she made a mistake, you're dating from the wound.

The antidote is radical acceptance of your divorce. Not approval — acceptance. Your marriage ended. You both played a role. Now you get to choose what you do with that experience.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Acceptance, not approval"]

**Step 3: Date from Your Values, Not Your Triggers**

Before your next date, write down your top five values. Not what you think women want to hear — what actually matters to you. Family. Integrity. Growth. Adventure. Whatever they are.

Then, during the date, notice if you're connecting over shared values or if you're just reacting to chemistry. Chemistry often means familiar dysfunction. Compatibility means shared values and secure attachment.

**Step 4: Practice Emotional Regulation**

When something triggers you on a date — she mentions her ex, she seems distracted, whatever — don't react immediately. Take a breath. Ask yourself: "Is this about her, or is this about my wound?"

Most of the time, it's about your wound. And that's okay. The goal isn't to never get triggered. The goal is to respond instead of react.

**Step 5: Get Professional Help**

Look, you can try to figure this out alone, but post-divorce dating requires specialized expertise. You wouldn't try to perform surgery on yourself. Don't try to heal attachment wounds without guidance.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Get professional help"]

The work I do with clients goes way deeper than these five steps. We spend weeks identifying attachment patterns, healing divorce trauma, and practicing new relationship skills in real-world situations.

---

## 7. CTA (15:00-End)
**[Word Count: 300]**

So remember — it's not about getting better matches. It's about healing your attachment wounds so you naturally attract emotionally healthy women.

When you do this work, everything changes. You stop feeling invisible because you're no longer hiding behind your wounds. You stop attracting the wrong women because you're no longer dating from the wrong energy. You start feeling hopeful about love again because you finally understand how it actually works.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Hopeful, selective, confident"]

You become what I call The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man — a guy who attracts the right women because he finally knows who he is. Your kids see you in a healthy relationship. You wake up next to someone who chose you, not settled for you. You're at peace with your past and excited about your future.

If you want to dive deeper into this, I've created a free assessment called the Second-Act Dating Audit. It'll help you identify exactly what's keeping you stuck and give you a personalized roadmap for breaking these patterns.

The link is in the description below, or you can go to secondactdating.com/audit.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "secondactdating.com/audit"]

For those of you ready to do this work with expert guidance, I also work with a small group of men through The Second Act Method. It's a six-month program where we heal your attachment wounds and teach you to attract the right women. There's a link in the description for that too.

Hit subscribe if this resonated with you. I post weekly content for divorced men who refuse to settle for loneliness.

And drop a comment below — what's your biggest takeaway from this video? I read every single comment.

Thanks for watching, and I'll see you in the next one.

[END SCREEN: Subscribe button, related videos, audit link]

---

## Carousel #8 — Educational (MOF)
**Carousel Title:** 5 Signs You're Dating from Your Wound (Not Your Wholeness)
**Core Belief Targeted:** Belief #2 (Real Problem) — Self-awareness of current patterns
**Funnel Stage:** MOF

### Slide 1
**Headline Text:** Are You Dating from Your Wound?
**Body Text:** 5 signs you're dating to prove your ex wrong instead of finding the right woman.
**Visual Direction:** Question mark background, thought-provoking imagery

### Slide 2
**Headline Text:** Sign #1: You're Angry
**Body Text:** Every woman reminds you of your ex. You're suspicious of motives. You feel defensive on every date.
**Visual Direction:** Red background, angry face or storm icon, white text

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** Sign #2: You're Desperate
**Body Text:** You'll take anyone who shows interest. You ignore red flags. You're afraid of being alone more than being wrong.
**Visual Direction:** Orange background, warning triangle, white text

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** Sign #3: You're Reactive
**Body Text:** Small triggers create big responses. You overreact to normal relationship challenges. Past wounds control present choices.
**Visual Direction:** Yellow background, explosion icon, dark text

**Body Text:** You date to show your ex you're desirable. You choose partners based on what others will think, not what you feel.

### Slide 7
**Headline Text:** The Solution
**Body Text:** The Second Act Method heals the wound first, then teaches you to date from wholeness.

**Caption:**
After my divorce in 2014, I spent 18 months dating from my wound without realizing it.

I was angry, suspicious, and kept attracting women who reminded me of my ex. I thought the problem was the dating apps, the women, the timing — everything except what it actually was.

My mentor finally called me out: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong."

That broke me open because it was true.

Most divorced men date from their wounds without knowing it. They think they're healed because they're not crying every day, but emotional reactivity tells a different story.

If you recognize yourself in these 5 signs, you're not broken — you're just dating from hurt instead of healing. The Second Act Method changes that.

Let me be completely transparent about our results.

Out of our last 80 clients who completed The Second Act Method, 63 achieved meaningful relationship success. That's a 79% success rate.

Here's what "success" means: In a committed, healthy relationship with someone who chooses them (not settles for them). Dating from wholeness, not wounds. Confident in their ability to recognize and attract emotionally healthy women.

The timeline varies: Most men see their first wins within 30 days (2-3 meaningful dates and emotional shifts). Most find their person within 6-9 months of starting.

What about the 17 who didn't hit the mark? Some didn't complete the program. Others needed more time to heal deeper wounds. That's why we offer 12-month extensions — because every man's journey is different.

The data is clear: When divorced men do the inner work with expert guidance, they find love.

"Group programs won't work for me. My situation is too unique."

Every divorced man thinks this. I thought it too after my divorce in 2014.

Here's what I learned: Shame thrives in isolation. When you're alone with your thoughts, your problems feel uniquely broken. When you're in community with other men who've walked the same path, you realize you're not special — you're human.

Individual therapy helps you process the past. Group coaching helps you build the future.

In our Brotherhood community of 3000 men, shame dies and growth accelerates. You see other men's breakthroughs and think "if he can do it, so can I." You get called out on your BS by men who've used the same excuses. You have accountability for your dating behavior, not just your feelings about it.

Mike D. avoided dating for 18 months trying to "figure himself out" alone. Within 11 months of joining the Brotherhood, he found his person. "I wasn't broken. I was healing — but I needed the right community to heal effectively."

relationship-dense.content:
# SHORT-FORM VIDEO SCRIPTS
## Second Act Dating — David Alvarez

---

## Short #1 — Hook Clip
**Type:** Pattern Interrupt Hook  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts

### Hook
"Stop trying to get better matches. Here's why you keep attracting your ex-wife."

### Body Script
Stop trying to get better matches. The problem isn't your photos or your bio.

You're 47, successful, and you haven't had a second date in eight months. Every woman either ghosts you or reminds you of your ex.

Here's the brutal truth: You attract what you are, not what you want.

If you're dating from anger, you attract angry women. If you're dating to prove your ex wrong, you attract women who are also trying to prove something.

You're not broken. You're just dating from the wound instead of from wholeness.

The guys who figure this out? They stop feeling invisible. They stop attracting the wrong women. They finally understand how love actually works.

### On-Screen Text
- "STOP trying to get better matches"
- "You attract what you are"
- "Dating from the wound vs wholeness"

### CTA/Closing
"Comment 'WOUND' if this hit different."

### Suggested Visual
Talking head with text overlays. Cut between close-up and medium shots for emphasis.

### Hashtags
#DivorcedDads #DatingOver45 #SecondActDating #MentalHealthMatters #AttachmentTheory

### Estimated Duration
45 seconds

---

## Short #2 — Belief Shift Clip
**Type:** Core Belief Challenge  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts

### Hook
"Your ex moved on first. Here's why that's actually good news."

### Body Script
Your ex-wife is probably in a relationship already, posting photos with her new partner, while you're swiping through the same profiles every night.

That pisses you off, right?

Here's what I need you to understand: She moved on fast because she didn't do the work. She just found someone new to distract her from the same patterns.

You? You're taking time to heal. You're learning to date from wholeness instead of wounds.

That's why it's taking longer. That's why it feels harder.

But when you find your person, it'll be real love. Not just familiar dysfunction.

The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man doesn't rush into the next relationship. He builds the foundation for the last one.

### On-Screen Text
- "Your ex moved on first"
- "She didn't do the work"
- "You're building the foundation"
- "For the LAST relationship"

### CTA/Closing
"Follow for more uncomfortable truths about post-divorce dating."

### Suggested Visual
Talking head with phone screen B-roll showing dating apps, then transition back to direct camera address.

### Hashtags
#DivorcedMen #DatingAfterDivorce #EmotionalIntelligence #HealthyRelationships #SecondActDating

### Estimated Duration
50 seconds

---

## Short #3 — Story Clip
**Type:** Personal Transformation Story  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts

### Hook
"I was 38, making $180K, and sleeping on a mattress on the floor."

### Body Script
After my divorce in 2014, I spent 18 months dating badly. I was angry, distrustful, and kept attracting women who reminded me of my ex.

I was 38 years old, making $180K in a private therapy practice, and still sleeping on a mattress on the floor of a rental.

Then my mentor told me something that broke me open: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails."

I did 2 years of my own therapy. Real therapy, not just venting about my ex.

Today I have a 3000-person community, run 6 retreats per year, and remarried in 2021 to a woman I met in my own program.

The difference? I learned to date from wholeness, not wounds.

### On-Screen Text
- "$180K but sleeping on a mattress"
- "Dating to prove my ex wrong"
- "2 years of real therapy"
- "Remarried 2021"

### CTA/Closing
"Link in bio for the free Second-Act Dating Audit."

### Suggested Visual
Talking head with lifestyle B-roll (empty apartment, therapy session, wedding photo). Keep cuts tight and energetic.

### Hashtags
#DatingAfterDivorce #PersonalGrowth #TherapyWorks #SecondActDating #MensMentalHealth

### Estimated Duration
55 seconds

---

## Short #4 — Belief Shift Clip
**Type:** Reframe Challenge  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts

### Hook
"Quality women aren't rejecting you. They're protecting themselves."

### Body Script
You feel invisible to quality women, right? Like they can't even see you exist?

Here's the reframe that'll mess with your head: They can see you perfectly. That's exactly why they're not interested.

When you're dating from wounds, emotionally healthy women can sense it immediately. They're not rejecting you personally — they're protecting themselves from your unhealed trauma.

That's actually their emotional intelligence working correctly.

A wounded man attracts wounded women. A whole man attracts whole women.

The good news? You're not broken. You're just broadcasting the wrong signal.

Fix the signal, change the reception.

### On-Screen Text
- "Quality women see you perfectly"
- "They're protecting themselves"
- "From your unhealed trauma"
- "Fix the signal, change the reception"

### CTA/Closing
"Comment 'SIGNAL' if you're ready to change yours."

### Suggested Visual
Talking head with split-screen effect showing "wounded energy" vs "whole energy" through body language and facial expressions.

### Hashtags
#DatingMindset #EmotionalIntelligence #AttachmentTheory #HealthyDating #SelfAwareness

### Estimated Duration
40 seconds

---

## Short #5 — Hook Clip
**Type:** Contrarian Take  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts

### Hook
"Dating apps aren't broken. You are."

### Body Script
You're burning $400 to $600 a month on premium subscriptions, thinking if you just optimize your approach, you'll finally get better matches.

But here's the brutal truth: You're 47, successful, and you haven't had a second date in eight months.

The apps aren't broken. Your attachment style is.

If better photos were the solution, every man with professional headshots would be in a happy relationship. But we both know that's not true.

You don't need better matches. You need to become the type of man who attracts emotionally healthy women.

That's internal work, not external optimization.

### On-Screen Text
- "Dating apps aren't broken"
- "$400-600/month burned"
- "Your attachment style is broken"
- "Internal work, not external optimization"

### CTA/Closing
"Follow if you're ready to do the real work."

### Suggested Visual
Talking head with phone screen B-roll showing dating app interfaces, premium subscription screens, then back to direct camera.

### Hashtags
#DatingApps #AttachmentTheory #DatingOver45 #SelfImprovement #RealTalk

### Estimated Duration
45 seconds

---

## Short #6 — CTA Clip
**Type:** Direct Action Driver  
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts

### Hook
"I've coached 420 men through post-divorce dating. Here's the pattern."

### Body Script
I've coached 420 men through post-divorce dating over 9 years. Built and sold a dating site for $1.2M. Remarried in 2021.

Here's the pattern I see every single time:

The men who stay stuck keep optimizing their profiles. The men who find love optimize their attachment style.

Dr. James K. came to me after 3 years divorced with zero second dates in 18 months. Six months later? Engaged to a woman he met at our retreat.

Carlos stopped dating women 15 years younger and found an age-appropriate partner he's been with for 9 months.

The difference? They learned to date from wholeness, not wounds.

If you're a divorced man over 45 who's tired of feeling invisible, I created a free assessment that'll show you exactly what's keeping you stuck.

### On-Screen Text
- "420 men coached"
- "Sold dating site for $1.2M"
- "Optimize attachment style"
- "Free assessment"

### CTA/Closing
"Link in bio for the Second-Act Dating Audit. This is only for men ready to do the work."

### Suggested Visual
Talking head with client success story graphics and testimonial overlays. End with clear CTA graphic.

### Hashtags
#SecondActDating #DatingCoach #DivorcedDads #AttachmentTheory #DatingAudit

### Estimated Duration
60 seconds
